observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant comply with the NGAS Manual for LGUs
observations.managementAction: prepare the required post-closing trial balance and financial statements of the 20% Development and submit the same to COA.

observations.recommendation: We commended the Management with the good practices of intact collections and deposits and continuous adherence with the guidelines, rules and regulations of cash management. We recommended that the commendable performance be continued and come up with a set time for the collectors to remit their collections daily. We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive monitor / supervise regularly the remittances of the collectors and deposits of the liquidating officer and collectors.

We commended the Management with the good practice of timely liquidation of all cash advances and leaving no balance at the end of the year. We recommended that the Disbursing Officer to continue the practice of following the guidelines in the grant, utilization and liquidation and reconciliation as the need arises in order to account the government funds. We further recommended that the Management and Municipal Treasurer continue the outstanding supervision to all of the AOs of the municipality to set a good example to other agencies.

We recommended that the Management estimate the annual budget for Special Education Fund based on the trends on the LGU’s actual and realized Special Education Tax revenue, and to exert efforts in the collection of all due RPTs of the Municipality.  The Management thru the Municipal Treasurer’s Office should initiate a more aggressive tax campaign to ensure a more efficient and higher collection of tax revenues.

We commended the Management of its good record of implementing the appropriated amounts of the 20% development fund at the optimum by implementing and accomplishing the lined-up projects contained in the AIP.  We recommended that the management continue the good practices and reprogram to priority PPAs the savings from implemented projects.

We commended the Management for the continuous adherence with the laws, policies and guidelines with regard to the allocation, utilization, accounting and reporting of DRRM funds. We recommended that the Management implement projects at its optimum in line with the set guidelines to build a resilient community. Reprogram the parts of the AIP for PPAs that are no longer needed to address the need of the Municipality and its constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise:
The Accounting Office to contact the LGU’s accounting system provider to add a function that could generate reports pertaining to GAD-related expenses and other GAD-related financial transactions, and that could monitor and properly account for the GAD fund; and
The Budget Office to fully computerize the monitoring of individual budget/fund of the LGU.

We recommended that the Management require Toyota Isabela Inc. to 1.) refund the amount paid in case the unit is not yet delivered, 2.) impose liquidated damages in accordance with the Procurement Law and 3.) Forfeit the retention fee due to Toyota in the amount of P23,192.00.  Lastly, ensure that all disbursements are in accordance with rules and regulations, otherwise, payments made contrary thereof shall be disallowed in audit, if warranted.

We recommended that the Management identify meticulously the mode of procurement to be indicated in the APP. Procurement of fuel, oil and lubricants must be done in public bidding considering the amount budgeted for the year which could give more advantage to the government through the generation of savings that could augment the Municipal coffers for other projects, programs and activities. However, if two (2) failed biddings occurred, negotiated procurement or direct contracting may be used.

We recommended that the Personnel-in-Charge ensure that the contracts provide the necessary contents and are executed within the prescribed period. Moreover, it is recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee require the BAC Secretariat to strictly comply with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 to avoid the occurrence of the aforementioned lapses.
Furthermore, we recommended that the Management submit the aforementioned lacking documents to facilitate the audit of the transactions.

We recommended the Municipal Engineering Office to follow the Manual of Procedures for the Procurement of Infrastructure Projects, Volume 3 in preparing the POWs and Cost Estimates which will be the basis of the Approved Budget of the Contract (ABC) of infrastructure projects. Please be reminded that any excess expenditures in the future that were not in compliance with laws, rules and regulations are disallowable in audit.

We recommended that Management stop the payment of TAs to the officials concerned and those RATA recipients, although not issued with government vehicles, but actually using government service vehicles in the performance of their duties and functions.  Likewise, we recommended that Management require the concerned officials to submit justification, with complete supporting documents, why their TA should not be disallowed in audit.
We further recommended that Management instruct the drivers to properly fill out the Driver’s Trip Tickets to ensure which official/employee actually used the service vehicles of the Municipality.

We recommended the Management to stop the hiring of individual COS workers in the Office of Vice Mayor, Sangguniang Bayan and SB Secretary whose services are not aligned with powers, duties, and functions, mandate, mission, vision of the legislative body for this is considered unnecessary expense, thus considered as disallowable expenditure.  Also, we recommended the Human Resource and Management Officer to cater applicants for Contract of Service workers whose duties and responsibilities are in line with the mandate of the office and perform functions that are not part of the job description of the existing regular employees.

We recommended that Management stop the use of Acknowledgement Receipt as a documentary requirement to its purchases instead ask for official receipt/sales invoice to support the existence of a valid transaction thus, transactions of similar in nature be suspended in future audit.

We recommended that the Management attach all the documentary requirements as prescribed by COA Circular No. 2012-001 for all the disbursements of the Municipality to prove the claim and to become valid.

We recommended that Management thru the Supply Officer cause the immediate marking of all the units of motor vehicles with the word “For Official Use Only” (3 inches) with the name of the office operating or using the same as required under the Manual on the Audit of Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles. Likewise, secure / follow up the government plates from the Land Transportation Office (LTO) or from the supplier of the newly procured vehicles.

We recommended the Management to revisit the grant of financial assistance to other LGUs especially to those belonging to higher income classification considering the financial capacity of the LGU.  If warranted, Management may also make proper representation with the other LGUs in the Province of Nueva Vizcaya for the evaluation of the practice on exchange of financial assistance between and among the LGUs in the province.

observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office inquired to the bank the debit and credit memos and to payees with stale checks and identified some adjustments, however, in CY 2022 there were still unadjusted debit and credit memos and outstanding stale checks for 2 years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (1) analyze the breakdown of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds of P107,010.00 and P698,069.94, respectively, and determine the amount which are collectible from or payable to the General Fund and/or from the Special Education Fund which is an initial step to conclude with the collection of the TF receivables from these two funds to improve the availability of cash for an added P107,010.00; and at the same time determine the amount to be parted from the Trust Fund in settlement to the GF and SEF (2) Analyze the composition and strategize the possible collectivity of the Due from Officers and Employees of P57,294.68, Accounts Receivable of P15,119.60, Advances for Officers and Employees of P18,001.54 and Other Receivables of P56,000.00; (3) Install internal control measures and policies to safeguard the disbursements of the Trust Funds for the stated purposes only; (4) Reconcile the Due to LGUs to come up with the proper amount accountable to the barangay shares under the Liga; and (5) the Trust Fund should once and for all eliminate the Due from and Due to Other Funds reciprocal accounts by rendering the payment through the fund transfer to the proper Funds the amounts due net of the Due from Other Funds receivables; these recommendations all to be acted upon in order to increase cash reserves to be used to implement trust purposes for grants from other government agencies specially lodged in the Trust Fund.
observations.managementAction: There were receivables that were collected in CY 2022 and collections are still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management (1) take note of all the errors in the output of the accounting system, (2) seek the assistance of the contracted service provider and explain/inform them of these erroneous outputs and processes, (3) redo the inputs with errors and ensure that outputs already comply with accounting standards, and (4) revisit the terms of the contract with the service provider and update the contract provisions to secure the best interest of the Municipality on the efficiency and effectiveness of the accounting system.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office addressed the issues together with the service provider of the accounting system and necessary adjustments were made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management finally stop disbursing the moneys of the Development Fund for wages of laborers not contributing to the acquisition of physical assets. Since Management had been instructed on the proper utilization of the DF and had been warned several times in the past years to stop charging these classification of expenses from the DF, yet, it continued violating the provisions of the JMC, result of audit would be to disallow these expenses and the approving authorities shall be liable to return back to the municipal coffers the misused moneys of the Development Fund to be utilized to further the development of the community. The day-to-day costs of maintaining the WATSAN and the MTPDO Forest Park should be taken by Management from the regular MOOE of the General Fund instead of charging them from the funds of the 20% DF.
observations.managementAction: Utilization of DF in CY 2022 were in accordance with the guidelines issued in CY 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that on Numerous Fund Transfers due to errors- (1) the Municipal Accountant assigns the bank account number of the proper checking account from which the voucher pertaining to calamity expenses should be paid from; (2) the Municipal Treasurer follow the assigned bank account number in preparing check payment; 

We also recommended that on erroneous use of account in the JEV- The Municipal Accountant always prepare a correct and proper Journal Entry Voucher for every transaction of the Municipality in order to present the correct accounts that truly represent the actual transactions that transpired which are summarized in reporting terms required under the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS). In that way, the financial position and results of operation whether monthly or interim are properly presented that enhance the decision of stakeholders and interpretation of the users of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office adjusted the errors made and assured that entries made in CY 2022 were proper. The Treasury Office already assigned the bank account number in the preparation of checks.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management should establish the proper systems and procedures of accounting for and managing supplies and materials by lodging them first in the Inventory account upon acceptance of delivery, recording the new stocks in the Supplies Ledger Cards (SLCs) by the Accounting Office upon recording in the JEV, recording in the Stock Cards by the GSO of the receipt and consequent issuances and maintaining proper costing using the Weighted Average Method. Management should provide for the position of a focal person or a General Services Officer who shall manage the procurement and distribution of Inventory of supplies and materials, stockpile, and motor-pool supplies.
observations.managementAction:
Due to the transition period from the treasury office to the supply officer staggered implementation were being made with regard to the management of supplies.
With regard to the recording of supplies, the Accounting Office recorded already their supplies in Asset Method.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management should be more prudent in the discharge of its functions and abide by the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-003 and avoid incurring unnecessary expenses prospectively. Management is advised to use information dissemination by educating the constituents through positive reinforcement and cause and effect approach for which if explained well would be a more fitting remembrance of the administration of the officials and employees rather than fleeting and short-term rewards for obedience to laws. Audit action whether to disallow or not to disallow these unnecessary expenses would depend on the justification to be submitted and the impact of the amount involved on the finances of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office, in their pre-audit function, made sure that disbursement of government funds was in line with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-003.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer issues official receipt for the collection of shares from the PCSO to be properly accounted as Income under the General Fund for appropriation in compliance with the above stated COA Circulars. Submit reason and basis for adopting recording under the Trust Fund
observations.managementAction: For CY 2022 no share for PCSO was received, however, Management assured that in the receipt of this kind of income, same will be recorded in the General Fund.

observations.recommendation: We commended the LGU management for its worthy accomplishments during the year as we encouraged them to continue contributing their best efforts to render the best and timely services to the people through the implementation of the projects, plans, programs and activities provided in the annual budget to be utilized in alignment to the purposes of the 20% Development Fund, Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund and the Gender and Development Fund. Management is also encouraged to obtain feedback from the citizens on their particular needs, conduct surveys to identify priority development programs and seek other sources of funds to implement these plans. The agencies that supervise LGUs may be able to consider this municipal achievement and grant commensurate reward for implementing the majority of its intended activities as laid in the annual Local Expenditure Program.
observations.managementAction: Management maintained its high record in the implementation of PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management (1) procure their common-use supplies directly from the PS-DBM or any of its depots; otherwise (2) conduct public bidding through the use of the PhilGEPS as the central portal of government procurement transactions; (3) provide clerical manpower to assist in compliance with the posting of awards and contracts in the PhilGEPS, (4) register with and conduct their procurement using the Virtual Store, and  (5) craft the Municipal Procurement Operations Manual in accordance with AO No./ 17 and GPPB Resolution 20-2018 to attain transparency, economy and efficiency in all their procurement activities.
observations.managementAction: Management already procured their supplies in PS-DBM. Only the supplies that cannot be provided by the PS-DBM were purchased from other supplier through shopping.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that in all infrastructure contracts even if below the P200,000 mark approved budget for the contract, Management should (1) prepare and serve the Notice to Proceed to contractors to show reckoning date on when to start the project and the project duration, (2) require the contracted properties to provide the Performance Bond before the execution of the contract, and (3) require the contractor to submit Warranty Surety Bond to secure the project after initial acceptance by Management against structural defects and deficiencies.
observations.managementAction: Management tried to implement the recommendations however, no date was indicated in the issuance of NTP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management should (1) indicate varying number of days and period within which to implement the Memorandum of Agreement depending on the actual purpose of the barangay based on the Program of Works of the project; (2) assign a focal person in the Engineering office to conduct ocular inspection of the accomplishments in the barangays, and (3) prepare and submit a written report to the Mayor noted by the Municipal Engineer on the result  of monitoring of each of the barangay projects funded from the municipal 20% Development Fund. In this way, government funds shall be utilized efficiently to serve the immediate need for economic and social welfare of the barangay constituents. Further, the delay in the submission of liquidation of barangay projects is prevented which paves the way to aid the barangay government in planning early for the inclusion in the municipal budget of the annual proposal for another grant of the municipal equal share.
observations.managementAction: Management revised the MOA per barangay when it comes to the implementation of projects. An engineer was designated as a focal person for Barangays.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the BAC should apply the post qualification of the bidder with the LCB in all initial payments of contracts to provide adequate information that justify why the bidder should be declared with the LCB. 

We recommended that the BAC prepare and attach to the related vouchers the Invitation to pre-selected suppliers when the Limited Source Bidding alternative method of procurement is used. Please attach also the pre-selection list which should be at least three (3) suppliers/contractors but does not mean to limit the selection to only three (3) prominent local suppliers to avoid the notion of giving undue favor but select all the suppliers who can deliver goods and services based on financial, technical and legal capability to execute the procurement contract.

We recommended that the Accountant require the Accomplishment Report together with the Daily Time Record to be submitted by the Doctor to the Barrio, Day Care Workers, Barangay Nutrition Scholars and Barangay Health Workers for accountability and transparency purposes in support of the disbursement of public funds. 

We recommended that the Accounting office should maintain the file of Waste Materials Reports and Equipment Repairs and Maintenance Cards to assist Management in decision making on the disposition of municipal properties. 

We recommended that Management should (1) prepare project proposals based on realistic estimates of attendees such that these persons are actually the preferred targets due to qualification and sectoral interest based on  the purpose of the activity, (2) assign an accountable person who should certify on the veracity of the attendance sheet to preclude doubts and enhance reliability of information, and (3) schedule the activities based on the availability venue and the condition of the weather which affects the willingness of persons to join and participate. Actual pictures in different angles should also be submitted as a relevant proof that the activity was conducted.  

We recommended that Management should require that all Purchase Orders should have complete indication of date on the “Conforme” portion as the date from which to start counting the number of days of delay for which the Supplier is accountable to pay Liquidated Damages for failure to deliver goods and services on the agreed time period. This clause as provided in the Purchase Order has the purpose to prevent any untoward loss to life and property due to delay in delivery by the supplier. 

We recommended that the focal person in charge of processing the grant of travel authority for all municipal vehicles should verify and check that all the required information in the Driver’s Trip Ticket are properly filled-up including the odometer reading for fuel beginning, fuel requisitioned, fuel consumption and fuel balance at the end of the trip. The Office accountable for the public vehicle should be indicated in the fuel consumption report aside from the plate number for easy identification of requisitioner. All defective odometers of municipal vehicles should be submitted for repair or replacement. Assign a gate guard who will serve as Inspector for every going-out and coming-in of municipal public vehicles. The guard should maintain a logbook to record the movements of municipal government vehicles. He should also record the details of the odometer readings before trip and after the trip. Moreover, the GSO should process the conversion of private plates into red plates on all government vehicles are required by laws, rules and regulations. 

We recommended that the concerned municipal officials and employees should exercise prudence and discretion on the use of public money and property and maintain telephone usage within the allowed limit to prevent overconsumption, unless property justified. This observation is to remind concerned persons to use government money and property within the allowed authority, and going beyond that is already abuse and wasteful use of government resources. 

We recommended that the Engineering office provide for daily attendance sheets for laborer for all projects implemented by administration which should be accomplished personally by each of the laborers such that “No Work-No Pay policy” should be applied to an absentee laborer. This is to document actual attendance and prevent the impression that “ghost” persons might be employed for the job which should not be the case.
observations.managementAction: Management tried to implement all the recommendations, however, there were transactions and documentary requirements that needs improvement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management should allocate a separate appropriation for the Office of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer under the General Fund for maintenance and other operating expenses from which they could properly charge the cost of repairs and maintenance of ambulance and rescue vehicles and the honoraria of the rescuers and BHERTs in order to keep intact the 5% LDRRMF for its intended purposes as provided above in the JMC 2013-1. Stop these claims from being disburse in the 5%LDRRMF and the STF-LDRRMF as this is considered irregular and disallowable consistent with COA Circular No. 2012-003.
observations.managementAction: All transactions budgeted and charged to the LDRRMF were in line with the guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU management strictly comply with the prescribed guidelines in the grant and payment of Collective Negotiation Agreement (CNA) as provided in Section 4.1.2(2) of DBM Budget Circular No. 2021-3. Management is enjoined to submit to the Team valid documents and accomplishment report to substantiate its entitlement of the CNA in CY 2021 for proper evaluation.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted documents and accomplishment reports to substantiated their entitlement of CNA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (1) initiate the processing of transfer of ownership/ titling of the municipal real properties which are not yet secures with Certificates of Titles; (2) determine the acquisition cost of these land properties to be reconciled with the records of the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Accountant and effect any adjustment whenever required; and (3) see to it that this recommendation is fully implemented in FY 2022 in order to secure the best interest of the Municipal Government over its ownership on its real properties and to fully substantiate the existence of the Land account amounting to P2,428,036.46 in the municipal books.
observations.managementAction: Management secured documents to be used in the transfer of ownership or titling of land.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (1) pay only on duly supported and authorized transactions, and (2) present in complete forms the supporting documents and computations of benefits from national government funds, so that transactions pass in audit because they are found to be complete and in order in compliance with the basic requirements for transactions as prescribed in PPD 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted all the supporting documents and computations to support their claims of Hazard Pay.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, through its Inventory Committee, conduct physical count of inventories and properties of the Municipality, reconcile the results of the count with the accounting records and submit the complete and accurate RPCI and RPCPPE to the Audit Team, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1.
observations.managementAction: The Supply Officer has initiated the conduct of physical count of properties some time in June 2021.
observations.reason: The actions taken by the Management did not correspond to partial compliance with the audit recom-mendations.  Physical count of inventories and properties at year-end was not conducted by the Inventory Committee.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

Local Finance Committee identify additional sources of income;

Municipal Treasurer (i) determine doable measures to improve collection efficiency of internally-sourced taxes, fees and charges; (ii) keep a master list of stallholders and indicate therein the vital information such as stall/table number, description of location whether it is inside, outside or corner stall, stall area, rate per square meter or rate per day; and (iii) maintain a subsidiary ledger of each stallholder; and

Local Chief Executive execute a new contract of lease with the seven stallholders whose lease contracts has already expired.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by Management.
observations.reason: Verification of the Statement of Financial Performance for CY 2021 of the Municipality showed that the Share from the IRA still constitutes a greater portion of its total revenues equivalent to 95.31 percent.  This is found higher than 94.49 percent which was the percentage computed last year.  This only showed that the Municipality is still dependent on its share from IRA to sustain its operations and support its programs and projects.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to exert extra efforts to identify the breakdown of P254,194.30 balance from 2007.  Send demand letters to identified accountable officers and pursue the liquidation or settlement thereof through refund and/or withholding of salaries.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by Management.
observations.reason: The balance amounting to P254,194.30 pertaining to prior years’ cash advances remained unliquidated  at year-end.  This still formed part of the balance of the Advances to Officers and Employees account as at December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor advise the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to enforce collection thereof to protect the interest of the government or request for authority from COA to write-off such long outstanding receivables through a Sanggunian Resolution, in line with COA Resolution No. 2003-002 dated January 30, 2003, stating the reasons for write-off and extent of follow-up made.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality collected P12,000.00 from Hon. Melania Alojado through salary deductions from January to December 2021.
observations.reason: The collection efficiency for the long outstanding accounts is very low.  Verification of the schedules as of December 31, 2021 submitted by the ICO-Municipal Accountant revealed that the dormant accounts with a long outstanding balance of P989,076.28 (net of the refund) still remained in the books under the Other Receivables account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management prioritize the implementation of projects, programs and activities that are identified in the SWM plan and to provide adequate funding to effectively implement relevant PPAs relative to solid waste management.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has pending loan application with Land Bank of the Philippines for priority projects. If granted, ₱60M is allocated for the whole Sewerage System Project as planned to finally realize an effective solid waste management.
observations.reason: Implementation of PPAs is still subject to availability of funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipality provide competent legal basis specifically authorizing the  granting of the Economic Resiliency Assistance (ERA) to LGUs, otherwise, the same shall be disallowed and returned back to the government coffers for having been disbursed in violation of government laws, rules and regulations.  The Municipality shall only grant additional personnel benefits as provided and mandated by government rules and regulations and by competent authority. The Municipality is also advised to refrain from duplicating the granting of benefit of same nature in future operations.
observations.managementAction: Refund not yet fully made.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that Management, through its Inventory Committee, conduct physical count of the inventories and properties of the Municipality, reconcile the results of the count with the accounting records and submit the complete and accurate RPCI and RPCPPE to the Audit Team, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1.

We recommended that the ICO-Municipal Accountant see to it that all the documentary requirements provided in COA Circular No. 2012-001 are complied with before payment will be made to the claimants to protect the interest of the government.
Moreover, we recommended that the Municipality stop the granting of advance payments since it violates Section 88 of P.D. No. 1445.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended that the:
MDRRMO and the Municipal Engineer (i) jointly evaluate the implementation of the projects classified as CO during the year.  If these were not implemented at year-end, include those in the LDRRMFIP and the Report on PPAs of the following year; and (ii) provide explanation on the reasons for the non-implementation of various PPAs with budgets totaling P550,000.00 classified as MOOE and failure to commence the actual works on the project – Construction/Repair of Riverbank Protection considering that the construction materials were already delivered on August 27, 2021;
MDRRMO (i) identify projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years; and (ii) include all the equipment purchased during the year in the template – Inventory of Equipment Purchased Out of DRRMF/Donations as of December 31, 2021; and
ICO-Municipal Accountant (i) coordinate with the Municipal Engineer and the MDRRMO on the submission of the lacking documents noted in this report; (ii) consider all the transactions affecting the LDRRMF in the disclosure provided in the Notes to Financial Statements including the funds transfers received by the Municipality and the disbursements relative thereto; and (iii) effect the adjusting entries in the books for the recognition on the completion of the project – Construction of Evacuation Center Management Office and Comfort Room with Water Supply Facility in the Trust Fund, transfer of the completed asset in the General Fund, and deduction in the Trust Liabilities - DRRMF account by an amount of P120,050.00 for the project – Procurement of NiEM Kits.
On the observations regarding procurement, we recommended that the:
BAC (i) ensure strict compliance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 to prevent the same lapses in future procurements; (ii) see to it that the information contained in the documents/reports being prepared are consistent; and (iii) ensure timely submission of the POs and the documents forming part thereof to the Audit Team in compliance with Item 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001; and
Inspectorate Committee carefully inspect the items that were procured upon delivery thereof by the concerned supplier as to the correctness of quantities, conformity with the technical specifications and absence of defects.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended that the:
Municipal Engineer (i) being one of the signatories in the SWA, carefully review the information reported by contractor, to be able to present accurate and reliable information to the users thereof; (ii) properly evaluate the documents (including the correctness of the information) submitted by the contractor to support his claim; and (iii) coordinate with the contractor on the submission of the lacking documents noted in this report; and
ICO-Municipal Accountant thoroughly check the compliance of the contractor with the documentary requirements enumerated in the COA Circular No. 2012-001 during the processing of claims.  If there were lacking documents noted during the evaluation, immediately require the contractor to submit the same before payments will be made.

We recommended that Management immediately transfer the remaining cash balance of ₱114,616.73 maintained with ASPAC Bank-Bantayan Branch to an authorized government depository bank as enumerated under DOF Department Circular No. 01-2017 and expedite the closure of said bank accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide for Allowance for Impairment to present fairly the Receivables accounts in the financial statements at net realizable amount consistent with the requirements of IPSAS 29.

Further, we recommended that the Management request from the Commission on Audit for authority to write-off of the Due from NGA, Due from GOCC and Due from LGU accounts totaling ₱5,578.23 in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to initiate the disposal of the unserviceable properties under Section 162 of COA Circular 92-386.

Further, direct the Municipal Accountant, after ascertaining the Recoverable Service Amount or the higher of Fair Value less Cost to Sell and Value in Use, set-up the allowance for impairment consistent with the requirements of IPSAS 21, to present fairly the PPE accounts in the financial statements.

Henceforth, we recommended that Management, through the BAC, require a duly accomplished statement of the bidder of all ongoing government and private contracts, conduct the post-qualification evaluation thoroughly, and ensure that all submitted eligibility requirements and statements are verified and checked in compliance with the procedures set forth under the RIRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly comply with the conditions and/or requirements on the use of alternative modes of procurement as set forth in R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR.

a) Submit the lacking documentary requirements and henceforth ensure that all disbursements are supported with complete documentation as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-001 and 2009-001 and comply with the RIRR of R.A. No. 9184.

b) Follow the standard formats for Invitation to Apply for Eligibility and Bid Form in compliance with Item 1.0 of Appendix 8 of the RIRR to encourage prospective bidders to participate.

We recommended that the BAC and TWG submit written explanation/justification on the noted deficiencies and corrective actions taken, without prejudice to the applicable penalties and sanctions as provided for by law.

Henceforth, we further recommended that the Management exercise due diligence in the conduct of its procurement and implement remedial measures to ensure compliance with R.A. No. 9184 and its updated implementing rules, regulations, and issuances.

We recommended that the Management follow the appropriate standard rates on the cost for the sale of the bidding documents in accordance with Appendix 8 of Revised IRR of RA 9184 in order to regulate its price and to encourage prospective bidders to participate.

We recommended that Management always ensure that priority projects are timely carried out, and the fund is optimally utilized with the view of effecting efficiency, economy and effectiveness in the disposition of government resources in compliance with the provisions outlined in DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 and 2020-1.

We recommended that Management:

Prepare the list of all on-going Government Projects/Programs/Activities at the beginning of the year in compliance with the COA Circular No. 2013-004 to avoid imposition of any penalties/sanctions related thereto.

Submit to the Audit Team a notification within ten days after the award of an infrastructure project that appropriate signboards and/or public notices have already been posted.

We recommended that Management submit justification on the subject expenditures for evaluation by the Audit Team. Further, we recommended that Management adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-003 to avoid the incurrence of unconscionable expenditures which are disallowable in audit.

We recommended that Management comply with the provisions of NDRRMC DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and ensure that MDRRM funds are spent in accordance with the rules and regulations intended for the proper utilization of the fund.

We recommended that Management submit a supplemental LDRRMFIP reflecting the changes thereto and effect its approval in accordance with the provisions set forth in R.A. No. 10121 and Its IRR and COA Circular No. 2012-002. Henceforth, revisit the pertinent laws, procedural rules and regulations on the release and utilization of the LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Management institute proper measures to ensure that PPAs in the GAD Plan and Budget are adequately implemented and continue to monitor compliance thereof with the provisions of PCW DILG DBM NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 in order to attain the GAD objectives.

2.6	We recommended that Management (1) analyze the breakdown of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds of P107,010.00 and P698,069.94, respectively, and determine the amount which are collectible from or payable to the General Fund and/or from the Special Education Fund which is an initial step to conclude with the collection of the TF receivables from these two funds to improve the availability of cash for an added P107,010.00; and at the same time determine the amount to be parted from the Trust Fund in settlement to the GF and SEF  (2) Analyze the composition and strategize the possible collectivity of the Due from Officers and Employees of P57,294.68, Accounts Receivable of P15,119.60, Advances for Officers and Employees of P18,001.54 and Other Receivables of P56,000.00; (3) Install internal control measures and policies to safeguard the disbursements of the Trust Funds for the stated purposes only; (4) Reconcile the Due to LGUs to come up with the proper amount accountable to the barangay shares under the Liga; and (5) the Trust Fund should once and for all eliminate the Due from and Due to Other Funds reciprocal accounts by rendering the payment through fund transfer to the proper Funds the amounts due net of the Due from Other Funds receivables;  these recommendations all to be acted upon in order to increase cash reserves to be used to implement trust purposes for grants from other government agencies specially lodged in the Trust Fund.

3.5	We recommended that Management (1) take note of all the errors in the output of the accounting system, (2) seek the assistance of the contracted service provider and explain /inform them of these erroneous outputs and processes, (3) redo the inputs with errors and ensure that outputs already comply with accounting standards,  and (4) revisit the terms of the contract with the service provider and update the contract provisions to secure the best interest of the Municipality on the efficiency and effectiveness of the accounting system.

4.10	We recommended that Management finally stop disbursing the moneys of the Development Fund for wages of laborers not contributing to the acquisition of physical assets. Since Management had been instructed on the proper utilization of the DF and had been warned  several times in the past years to stop charging these classification of expenses from the DF, yet, it continued violating the provisions of the JMC, result of audit would be to disallow these expenses and the approving authorities shall be liable to return back to the municipal coffers the misused moneys of the Development Fund to be utilized to further the development of the community. The day-to-day costs of maintaining the WATSAN and the MTPDO Forest Park should be taken by Management from the regular MOOE of the General Fund instead of charging them from the funds of the 20%DF.

5.11	We recommended that on Numerous Fund Transfers due to errors - (1) the Municipal Accountant assigns the bank account number of the proper checking account from which the voucher pertaining to calamity expenses should be paid from; (2) the Municipal Treasurer follow the assigned bank account number in preparing check payment;
5.12	We also recommended that on erroneous use of account in the JEV – The Municipal Accountant always prepare a correct and proper Journal Entry Voucher for every transaction of the Municipality in order to present the correct accounts that truly represent the actual transactions that transpired which are summarized in reporting terms required under the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS). In that way, the financial position and results of operation whether monthly or interim are properly presented that enhance the decision of stakeholders and interpretation of the users of the financial statements.

6.7	We recommended that Management should establish the proper systems and procedures of accounting for and managing supplies and materials by lodging them first in the Inventory account upon acceptance of delivery, recording the new stocks in the Supplies Ledger Cards (SLCs) by the Accounting Office upon recording in the JEV, recording in the Stock Cards by the GSO of the receipt and consequent issuances and maintaining proper costing using the Weighted Average Method. Management should provide for the position of a focal person or a General Services Officer who shall manage the procurement and distribution of Inventory of supplies and materials, stockpile and motorpool supplies.

7.8	We recommended that Management should be more prudent in the discharge of its functions and abide by the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-003 and avoid incurring unnecessary expenses prospectively. Management is advised to use information dissemination by educating the constituents through positive reinforcement and cause and effect approach for which if explained well would be a more fitting remembrance of the administration of the officials and employees rather than fleeting and short term rewards for obedience to laws. Audit action whether to disallow or not to disallow these unnecessary expenses would depend on the justification to be submitted and the impact of the amount involved on the finances of the Municipality.

8.5	We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer issue official receipt for the collection of shares from the PCSO to be properly accounted as Income under the General Fund for appropriation in compliance with the above stated COA Circulars. Submit reason and basis for adopting recording under the Trust Fund.

10.10	We recommended that Management (1) procure their common-use supplies directly from the PS-DBM or any of its depots; otherwise (2) conduct public bidding through the use the PhilGEPS as the central portal of government procurement transactions; (3) provide clerical manpower to assist in compliance with the posting of awards and contracts  in the PhilGEPS, (4) register with and conduct their procurement  using the Virtual Store, and (5) craft the Municipal Procurement Operations Manual in accordance with AO No. 17 and GPPB Resolution 20-2018 to attain transparency, economy and efficiency  in all their procurement activities.

11.9	We recommended that in all infrastructure contracts even if below the P200,000 mark approved budget for the contract, Management should (1) prepare and serve the Notice to Proceed to contractors to show reckoning date on when to start the project and the project duration, (2) require the contracted parties to provide the Performance Bond before the execution of  the contract, and (3) require the contractor to submit Warranty Surety Bond to secure the project after initial acceptance by Management against structural defects and deficiencies.

12.6	We recommended that Management should (1) indicate varying number of days and period within which to implement the Memorandum of Agreement depending on the actual purpose of the barangay based on the Program of Works of the project;  (2) assign a focal person in the Engineering Office to conduct ocular inspection of the accomplishments in the barangays, and (3) prepare and submit a written report to the Mayor noted by the Municipal Engineer on the result of monitoring of each of the barangay projects funded from the municipal 20% Development Fund. In this way, government funds shall be utilized efficiently to serve the immediate need for economic and social welfare of the barangay constituents. Further, the delay in the submission of liquidation of barangay projects is prevented which paves the way to aid the barangay government in planning early for the inclusion in the municipal budget the annual proposal for another grant of the municipal equal share.

13.8	We recommended that the BAC should supply the post qualification of the bidder with the LCB in all initial payments of contracts to provide adequate information that justify why the bidder should be declared with the LCB.
13.11	We recommended that the BAC prepare and attach to the related vouchers the Invitation to pre-selected suppliers when the Limited Source Bidding alternative method of procurement is used. Please attach also the pre-selection list which should be at least three (3) suppliers/contractors but does not mean to limit the selection to only three (3) prominent local suppliers to avoid the notion of giving undue favor but select all the suppliers who can deliver goods and services based on financial, technical and legal capability to execute  the procurement contract.
13.17	We recommended that the Accountant require the Accomplishment Report together with the Daily Time Record to be submitted by the Doctor to the Barrio, Day Care Workers, Barangay Nutrition Scholars and Barangay Health Workers for accountability and transparency purposes in support of the disbursement of public funds.
13.20	We recommended that the Accounting Office should maintain the file of Waste Materials Reports and the Equipment Repairs and Maintenance Cards to assist Management in decision making on the disposition of municipal properties.
13.22	We recommended that Management should (1) prepare project proposals based on realistic estimates of attendees such that these persons are actually the preferred targets due to qualification and sectoral interest based on the purpose of the activity, (2) assign an accountable person who should certify on the veracity of the attendance sheet to preclude doubts and enhance reliability of information, and (3)  schedule the activities based on the availability of venue and the condition of the weather which affects the willingness of persons to join and participate. Actual pictures in different angles should also be submitted as a relevant proof that the activity was conducted.
13.24	We recommended that Management should require that all Purchase Orders should have complete indication of date on the “Conforme” portion as the date from which to start counting the number of days of delay for which the Supplier is accountable to pay Liquidated Damages for failure to deliver goods and services on the agreed time period. This clause as provided in the Purchase Order has the purpose to prevent any untoward loss to life and property due to delay in delivery by the supplier.
13.26	We recommended that the focal person in charge of processing the grant of travel authority for all municipal vehicles should verify and check that all the required information in the Driver’s Trip Ticket are properly filled-up including the odometer reading for fuel beginning, fuel requisitioned, fuel consumption and fuel balance at the end of the trip. The Office accountable for the public vehicle should be indicated in the fuel consumption report aside from the plate number for easy identification of requisitioner. All defective odometers of municipal vehicles should be submitted for repair or replacement. Assign a gate guard who will serve as Inspector for every going-out and coming-in of municipal public vehicles. The guard should maintain a logbook to record the movements of municipal government vehicles. He should also record the details of the odometer readings before the trip and after the trip. Moreover, the GSO should process the conversion of private plates into red plates on all government vehicles as required by laws, rules and regulations.
13.28	We recommended that the concerned municipal officials and employees should exercise prudence and discretion on the use of public money and property and maintain telephone usage within the allowed limit to prevent overconsumption, unless properly justified. This observation is to remind concerned persons to use government money and property within the allowed authority, and going beyond that is already abuse and wasteful  use of government resources.
13.30	We recommended that the Engineering Office provide for daily attendance sheets for laborers for all projects implemented by administration which should be accomplished personally by each of the laborers such that “No Work-No Pay policy” should be applied to an absentee laborer. This is to document actual attendance and prevent the impression that ‘"ghost” persons might be employed for the job which should not be the case.
13.32	We recommended Management to submit the required documents per accomplished project to support the BAC honoraria such as :
Attendance to Pre-bid Conference
Attendance to bid openings
PhilGEPS posting
BAC Resolution on the conduct of bidding
Notice of Award
Post Qualification
Contract
Notice to Proceed
13.34	We recommended Management to require the concerned officials and employees to submit for auditorial review the Abstract of PhilGEPS posting of all bidded projects to substantiate the conduct of bidding or resulting alternative method of procurement if bidding fails twice.
13.36	We recommended Management to always attach the pictures of before, during and after implementation of projects to substantiate actual accomplishments made prior to the actual inspection and acceptance by the LGU and for the partial and final payment of contracts and even for wages for projects under administration.
13.38	We recommended Management to attach the pictures and minutes to meetings and gatherings to prove the occurrence of the event.
13.41	We recommended Management to require visitors to the Mayor’s Office to sign-in their attendance in paper to be used as supporting document to vouchers for meals and snacks to disclose the number of visitors and relate to the propriety of the actual expenses incurred.
13.44	Stop the grant of donations/cash assistance to private proprietors which is against laws, rules and regulations, instead, it must be prioritized for indigent constituents.
13.47	We recommended Management to refuse provisional receipts from suppliers, but require official receipts, sales invoices or acknowledgement receipts instead to prevent suspension of transactions.
13.49	We recommended Management to always observe transparency in reporting information on the utilization of the BGCM Funds by posting the corresponding reports in the LGU website and in three conspicuous places in the Municipality to provide stakeholders of the data on vital municipal actions pertaining to BGCM activities.
13.51	We recommended that Management always attach for validation the supporting documents for the change in the Target date of completion of projects such as Work Suspension Order and Variation Order for proper monitoring on the completion thereof and to clearly present the counting of dates in project implementation that would eventually lead to the final completion date.
13.53	We recommended Management to require the concerned officials and employees to submit for auditorial review the Abstract of PhilGEPS posting of all bidded projects to substantiate the conduct of bidding or resulting alternative method of procurement if bidding fails twice.
13.55	We recommended Management to always attach the copy of Disbursement vouchers where the retention fee reduced the amount claimed by the contractor to support all vouchers granting the request of the contractor for withdrawal of retention fee on completed projects.
13.57	We recommended Management to attach tabular distribution of cellcards indicating therein the quantity, cost per quantity and the total value per person for which the recipient signs receipt thereof in order to show the propriety of cellcard distribution.
13.59	We recommended that Management follow the policies in the use of government vehicles such as registering to red plates and placing the “For Official Use Only” sticker with the Official Seal of the Municipality to obtain security in all official travels and for proper monitoring and disclosure on ownership of government property.
13.61	We recommended Management to stop the reimbursement of meals and snacks during travel in lieu of the granting of complete per diems for all local travels in order to prevent double payment of meals paid in the per diems and at the same time reimbursed by the claimant. Government officials ferried by drivers who are not under the LGU Management have also their own travel per diems from their respective agencies.
13.63	We recommended that Management stop dispensing fuel to private vehicles used for official travels, instead, the official or employee who is granted authority to travel could only claim the actual fare for the distance travelled by the private vehicle from point of embarkation from official station, to the destination and back to the place of official station in order to prevent double payment of fuel and waste of government resources.
13.65	We recommended that Management always “certify correct” all the pages of all attendances in support of meals and snacks in order to acknowledge the truthfulness of the details and actual attendance of all persons indicated therein who attended the gathering and enforce Management’s assertion on existence.
13.67	We recommended Management to create policy on the amounts to be granted for financial assistance for funeral expenses citing therein the factors to be considered for the varying amounts to be supported by the details of the actual situation of the deceased and the bereaved family.
13.68	Management agreed to fully comply with the submission of observed deficiencies in municipal transactions in order to prevent the suspension or disallowance of the COA.

14.5	We recommended that Management should allocate a separate appropriation for the Office of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer under the General Fund for maintenance and other operating expenses from which they could properly charge the cost of repairs and maintenance of ambulance and rescue vehicles and the honoraria of the rescuers and BHERTs in order to keep intact the 5%LDRRMF for its intended purposes as provided above in the JMC 2013-1. Stop these claims from being disbursed in the 5%LDRRMF and the STF-LDRRMF as this is considered irregular and disallowable consistent with COA Circular No. 2012-003.

15.5	We recommended that the LGU management strictly comply with the prescribed guidelines in the grant and payment of Collective Negotiation Agreement (CNA) as provided in Section 4.1.2(2) of DBM Budget Circular No. 2021-3.  Management is enjoined to submit to the Team valid documents and accomplishment report to substantiate its entitlement to the CNA in CY 2021 for proper evaluation.

16.10	We recommended that Management (1) initiate the processing of the transfer of ownership/ titling of the municipal real properties which are not yet secured with Certificates of Titles; (2) determine the acquisition cost of these land properties to be reconciled with the records of the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Accountant and effect any adjustment whenever required; and (3) see to it that this recommendation is fully implemented in FY 2022 in order to secure the best interest of the Municipal Government over its ownership on its real properties and to fully substantiate the existence of the Land account amounting to P2,428,038.46 in the municipal books.

17.6	We recommended that Management (1) pay only on duly supported and authorized transactions, and (2) present in complete forms the supporting documents and computations of benefits from national government funds, so that transactions pass in audit because they are found to be complete and in order in compliance with the basic requirements to transactions as prescribed in PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

observations.managementAction: In June 2021, the MSWDO has submitted to the Audit Team the lacking distribution sheets for the payments of food and non-food supplies as well as multivitamins during CY 2020.  All the disbursements pertaining to COVID-19 Projects, Programs and Activities (PPAs) were accounted in the List of Vouchers and Summary of COVID-19 Fund Sources and Utilization submitted by the ICO-Municipal Accountant.  Verification also revealed that sense of urgency was demonstrated in the procurement procedures undertaken for the implementation of COVID-19 PPAs in CY 2021.
observations.reason: Audit of the funds utilized to finance COVID-19 PPAs disclosed that the Municipality failed to submit to the Audit Team the  Supplemental Annual Investment Program (AIP) for the PPAs charged against the 30 percent Quick Response Fund (QRF) for CY 2021.   Furthermore, the payment of the Active Hazard Duty Pay to the frontline Human Resources for Health amounting to P339,272.51 was not supported with the Memorandum of Agreement between the LGU and the DOH-CHD as well as the report on the number of COVID-19 patients attended to which are required under DBM-DOH Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2020 dated November 25, 2020.  The Audit Team likewise observed that the amount provided for COVID-19 PPAs out of the 70 percent Mitigation Fund for CY 2021 reported in the Summary of COVID-19 Fund Sources and Utilization was incorrect.  There were also instances when the BAC incurred delay in the posting of Notice of Award (NoA) and the Purchase Order (PO) in the PhilGEPS and LGU websites for contracts with Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) of above P50,000.00.   With regard to the submission of POs and their supporting documents to the Audit Team, delay was observed thereof (except for one project) which is not compliant with Item 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001.  Records of the Office also showed that the POs of two projects were not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.managementAction: The Management see to it that all the disbursements did not exceed the appropriations provided for the projects.  In April 2021, the Municipal Treasurer also effected a fund transfer amounting to P26,208.08 from the General Fund to the Trust Fund which should form part of the unexpended balance of 70 percent Mitigation Fund - MOOE for CY 2019.  An amount of P3,000,000.00 was also transferred to the Trust Fund in the same month representing the financial assistance to the Municipality which was affected by the flash flood caused by Typhoon “Ursula” from the Socio Civic Project Fund of the Office of the President.  This transaction was recorded as credit to Trust Liabilities - DRRMF account.  In November 2021, a fund transfer amounting to P120,050.00  was also made from the Trust Fund - Proper LBP Account No. 1162-1023-69 to General Fund LBP Account No. 1162-1019-90 representing the payment for the Procurement of NiEM Kits which should have been paid in the former bank account but were erroneously charged against the latter bank account.
observations.reason: The MDRRMO still failed to include in the Plan the information on the projects sourced out from the 70 percent Mitigation Fund - Capital Outlay (CO) for CY 2020 which were made continuing in CY 2021.   These were also not considered during the preparation of the Quarterly Report on PPAs for CY 2021 submitted by the Municipal Engineer.  Also, the BAC still incurred delay in the submission of some POs and their supporting documents to the Audit Team which is not compliant with Item 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001.

observations.managementAction: Out of the total appropriations amounting to P16,815,605.20, only two projects with appropriations totaling P4,500,000.00 equivalent to 26.76 percent were  not implemented at year-end.  This only showed that the Municipality see to it that majority of the PPAs charged against the 20 percent DF for CY 2021 were implemented during the year.   Evaluation of the manner of implementation on the projects disclosed that the works commenced after the construction materials were delivered by the concerned suppliers.  Minimal delay was observed though in the submission of POs and their supporting documents to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Various projects with appropriations totaling P7,680,940.00 were continuously not implemented during the year.  The Municipality also failed to commence actual construction on two projects despite delivery of materials.   Information on various continuing projects were likewise not disclosed in the List of Vouchers submitted by the ICO-Municipal Accountant.  The lacking photographs for the implementation of the projects – Rehabilitation of Municipal Roads (Poblacion) and Rehabilitation of Guintigbasan Multi-Purpose Hall in CY 2019 were still not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
On the procurement aspect, we recommended that the BAC strictly comply with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 in the conduct of procurement activities on the projects of the Municipality.    

Moreover, we recommended that the BAC carefully prepare the needed documents/reports in order to provide complete, correct and consistent information to the users thereof.
observations.managementAction: Auditorial review of the contracts and their supporting documents for the infrastructure projects namely: Rehabilitation/Improvement of Municipal Streets in Sitio Proper, Barangay Poblacion (labeled as Project No. 1) and Construction of Evacuation Center and Management Office and Comfort Room with Water Supply Facility (labeled as Project No. 2) with contract cost amounting to P9,218,013.74 and P2,997,263.50, respectively,     revealed substantial compliance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.   Minimal delay was observed though in the submission of PO and its supporting documents of Project No. 2.  On the other hand, the BAC incurred significant delay in the submission of PO and its supporting documents in Project No. 1.

The time elapsed to date reported in the Statement of Time Elapsed and Work Accomplished attached in the disbursement vouchers were correct.  Also, the warranty security was posted by the contractors before payments were made thereto.
observations.reason: The lacking photographs were still not submitted to the Audit Team.  Audit of the payments of infrastructure projects during the year revealed that some documentary requirements were not complied with.   Deficiencies were also noted in the documents supporting the claims.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the: 

Members of the GFPS (i) properly classify the PPAs into mandated and attributed programs in the GPB depending on the source of funds thereof.  If the programs are identified PPAs of other offices, classify those as attributed programs and administer the HGDG tests in order to determine the portion of the budgets that may be attributed to GAD.  Proper classification of the PPAs in the GPB is necessary in arriving at the correct appropriations for GAD Fund; (ii) see to it that the gender issues are properly indicated in the project briefs/ proposals of the identified GAD PPAs to be able to validate its inclusion in the GPB aside from results of the HGDG tests; (iii) ensure the correctness of the computation of the scores provided in the checklists to arrive at the correct amount of the budgets that may be attributed to GAD; (iv) submit explanation on the non-inclusion of the projects – Establishment of Fitness and Wellness Facility (Phase II), Establishment of Handwashing Facility and Sustainable Sanitation Program in the AIP; (v) ensure that the GAD Fund are properly utilized particularly on the conduct of GAD-related activities which involves procurement of catering services; (vi) thoroughly check the completeness of the attachments of GAD AR; and (vii) carefully prepare the Narrative ARs to be able to provide accurate and reliable information to the users thereof.  In doing this, see to it that consistent information are contained in the Narrative AR and other documents  attached in the disbursements of the GAD PPAs; 

BAC see to it that the POs and their supporting documents are submitted to the Audit Team within the period prescribed in COA Circular No. 2009-001; and
	
Municipal Accountant ensure the correctness of the disbursements accounted in the List of Vouchers/ Projects Charged Against the Fund since this will be used as the basis in the actual expenditures that will be forwarded in the GAD AR.
observations.managementAction: Computation made by the Audit Team showed that the Municipality satisfied the 5 percent minimum budgetary requirement for GAD Fund in CY 2021.   Separate appropriations amounting to P1,905,000.00 were also provided in the Annual Budget to cater the implementation of the mandated programs indicated in the GAD Plan and Budget (GPB).  With regard to the procurement of catering services, the concerned implementing unit ensured that the GAD Fund is properly utilized.   Minimal delay was observed though in the submission of POs and their supporting documents to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: There were incorrect and inconsistent information of various GAD PPAs noted in the related documents/ reports such as the GPB, Project Proposals, GAD Accomplishment Report (AR), Project ARs and the List of Vouchers.   Furthermore, the Audit Team observed several instances when the information contained in the aforementioned documents were not reconciled with the documents attached in the disbursement vouchers and payrolls relative to the implementation of the GAD PPAs.  The submitted GAD AR was also not supported with the actions taken by the LGU on the COA audit findings and recom-mendations as required in Section 4.1 (C.8) (5) of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.  In addition to this, other necessary documents such as the PIMME checklists, photographs, and Attendance Sheet were found to be lacking.

observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO properly classified the PPAs as to MOOE and CO in the LDRRMFIP for CY 2021.  He likewise ensured that all the disbursements paid out of the Fund are connected with disaster risk reduction and management activities.  In April 2021, the Municipal Treasurer also effected a fund transfer amounting to P26,208.08 from the General Fund to the Trust Fund which should form part of the unexpended balance of 70 percent Mitigation Fund - MOOE for CY 2019.  The amount P2,435,291.50 representing the unexpended balance of 30 percent QRF and 70 percent Mitigation Fund - MOOE for CY 2021 set-up at year-end to be transferred to the Special Trust Fund was correct.  It was further observed that all the disbursements pertaining to the LDRRMF were accounted in the Report on Utilization of DRRMF submitted by the ICO-Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: The MDRRMO did not identify projects and activities to be charged from the unexpended balances of 30 percent QRF and 70 percent Mitigation Fund - MOOE for CYs 2019 and 2020 in the LDRRMFIP for CY 2021.  He also failed to incorporate in the Plan the information on the projects sourced out from the 70 percent Mitigation Fund - CO for CY 2020 which were made continuing in CY 2021.  There were also lacking documents noted in the payments of wages of laborers for some projects charged against the Fund.   The fund transfers received by the Municipality and the disbursements charged against the projects identified therefrom were not disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements submitted by the ICO-Municipal Accountant.  Furthermore, the Municipal Engineer and the MPDC still failed to submit written explanation on the photographs attached in the payroll for the project – Furnishing and Improvement of Disaster Operation Center funded from the LDRRMF for CY 2017.  The Audit Team likewise observed some instances when the NoA was posted in the PhilGEPS and LGU websites beyond the prescribed period provided in Section IV (L) (2), Annex H of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 in the case of procurement undertaken through Negotiated Procurement - Emergency Cases.  The BAC still incurred delay in the submission of some POs and their supporting documents to the Audit Team which is not compliant with Item 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Development Council always ensure that the appropriations provided to support its development projects are sufficient as required by the existing laws, rules and regulations.
		
Moreover, we recommended that the MPDC and the Municipal Engineer: 

strictly comply with Items 2.0 and 3.0 of the JMC in the identification of projects to be charged against the 20% DF; 

carefully plan the imple-mentation of the projects and effectively execute the construction schedule to be able to timely deliver the services to the intended beneficiaries thereof; and

submit additional photographs to justify the final payment made to the laborers relative to the project – Rehabilitation of Guintigbasan Multi-Purpose Hall. 

We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer properly supervise the implementation of the projects which includes ensuring that the laborers who were hired for the actual construction thereof strictly follow the period of employment indicated in the respective Job Orders. 

The Municipal Accountant is also recommended to thoroughly check the completeness of the supporting documents attached in the claims.

We likewise recommended that the MPDC, Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant jointly coordinate in the preparation of reports to be able to present complete and reliable information to the users thereof.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2021, the Municipality has provided appropriations amounting to P16,815,605.20 for its development projects which is equivalent to 20 percent of the annual Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA).  Only two projects with appropriations totaling P4,500,000.00 were not implemented at year-end which constitutes 26.76 percent of the total appropriations  This only showed that the Municipality see to it that majority of the PPAs charged against the 20 percent DF for CY 2021 were implemented during the year.  Verification of the Daily Time Records of the laborers attached in the payrolls showed that they worked for the projects in accordance with the period of employment indicated in the Job Orders.  The Municipal Engineer also see to it that no laborers were paid twice on a particular day for the same project.  The Municipal Accountant also ensure that the payments of wages of laborers for the projects implemented by administration were completely supported with appropriate documents.  Minimal delay was observed though in the submission of POs and their supporting documents to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Various projects with appropriations totaling P7,680,940.00 were continuously not implemented during the year.  The ICO-Municipal Accountant also failed to include in the List of Vouchers for CY 2021 the information on several continuing projects.  The lacking photographs for the implementation of the projects – Rehabilitation of Municipal Roads (Poblacion) and Rehabilitation of Guintigbasan Multi-Purpose Hall in CY 2019 were still not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.managementAction: Auditorial review of the contracts and their supporting documents for the infrastructure projects namely: Rehabilitation/ Improvement of Municipal Streets in Sitio Proper, Barangay Poblacion (labeled as Project No. 1) and Construction of Evacuation Center and Management Office and Comfort Room with Water Supply Facility (labeled as Project No. 2) with contract cost amounting to P9,218,013.74 and P2,997,263.50, respectively,     revealed substantial compliance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.   Minimal delay was observed though in the submission of PO and its supporting documents of Project No. 2.  On the other hand, the BAC incurred significant delay in the submission of PO and its supporting documents in Project No. 1.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the persons liable or the Municipal Mayor in their behalf, file requests for installment payment to the PLO through the Audit Team on the remaining balance of the disallowance amounting to P305,192.05 in compliance with the aforementioned provisions.  
		
Moreover, we recommended that they see to it that the requests are completely supported with appropriate documents such as the proposed installment schedule on a per ND basis.  In the preparation of the said schedule, consider the provisions of Item 5 of COA Resolution No. 2017-021.
observations.managementAction: Refunds totaling P26,000.00 were made during the year.
observations.reason: As of this reporting, the Management still failed to file requests for installment payments on the disallowed allowances to the PLO of the Commission on Audit through the Audit Team.   The balance of the Receivables - Disallowances/ Charges account as at December 31, 2021 amounting to P179,520.93 pertains to the disallowances of the persons liable who are already separated from the service.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for all the LGU’s bank accounts to the Audit Team together with the paid checks, debit/credit memos for verification as provided for under Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-011.  Also make an extra effort to submit the bank reconciliation statements on time.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2018, the ICO-Municipal Accountant already prepared and submitted to the Audit Team the Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRSs) under the Special Education Fund for CYs 2010 to 2014 with Bank Account No. 1162-1024-58.
observations.reason: The BRSs under the Special Education Fund for the months of April 2014 to July 2014 were still not submitted to the Audit Team.  Also, significant delay was still observed by the Audit Team in the submission of the BRSs by the Municipal Accountant during CY 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
The Municipal Treasurer:

Instruct all collectors to prepare RCDs and remit all collections to the Municipal Treasurer in accordance with Section 29, Vol. 1 of the NGAS Manual;

Be more circumspect in the acknow-ledgment of remittances in terms of accuracy of the amount;

Refrain from allowing incomplete remittances from the collecting officers.

The Municipal Accountant:

Ensure the accuracy of the amounts of recorded transactions and adjust the balance of the accounts affected by the miscal-culated collections; and 

The practice of charging the unremitted collections to receivable account be refrained as this only encourages delayed remittance and/or incomplete remittance or non-remittance at all of the collections receipted by the collecting officers.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had already stopped the practice of recording unremitted collections by debiting the Due from Officers and Employees account.   Analysis of the General Ledger showing all the transactions affecting the Cash Local Treasury account for CY 2021 revealed that the Municipality exerted efforts in ensuring that intact deposit of collections was made.  This is evidenced by minimal balances of Cash Local Treasury account at the end of each month.
observations.reason: Verification of the Reports of Collections and Deposits for CY 2021 disclosed that the daily report includes collections on prior periods.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) comply with the mandatory posting of the updated APP, NOA, and other information relative to the Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act in the GPPB Online Portal as required in GPPB Circular 01-2020. Henceforth, we also recommended that the BAC continuously update themselves with the issuances and advisories of the GPPB and other related regulatory agencies on the conduct of procurement activities to avoid non-compliance thereof.
observations.managementAction: The management immediately updated the APP and posted the Emergency purchases in three conspicuous places. Online posting at GPPB was no longer possible though. BAC promised to be more keen on GPPB Circular 01-2020 provisions to promote the sense of accountability and transparency on future Emergency Procurement under Bayanihan Act.
