observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit a justification for the above noted deficiencies and ensure that the OSS are notarized accordingly. Henceforth, we recommended that the BAC continuously update themselves with the issuances and advisories of the GPPB and other related regulatory agencies on the conduct of procurement activities to avoid non-compliance thereof.
observations.managementAction: All documentations with discrepancies during the procurements under the Bayanihan Act were immediately addressed, corrected., and submitted to the COA Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management observe the procedural rules and the proper classifications in the accounting and recording of its supplies and properties in accordance with the New Government Accounting System and Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSASs) and other similar issuances to avoid financial misstatements.
observations.managementAction: COA observations on the observance of procedural rules on proper accounting and recording of supplies and properties are now corrected. All procurements of such welfare goods, medical supplies and all other purchases of the like are now coursed through the inventory account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the responsible officials submit a justification on the noted deficiency and the inclusion of ineligible beneficiaries as well as the lacking documentary requirements. If warranted, demand for a refund for funds illegally received and/or impose corresponding sanctions as provided for in Memorandum Circular No. 09 Series of 2020 dated April 9, 2020.
observations.managementAction: Liquidation and documentation having been submitted to the funding agency with all justifications in the determination of eligible recipients and the disbursements of the Social Amelioration Program, Management have not received any complaints from the DSWD Regional Office as to the manner of LGU implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prepare the supplemental LDRRMFIP reflecting the changes thereto and effect its approval in accordance with the provisions set forth in Item 5.1.3.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and Item 2.0 of DILG DBM JMC No. 02.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRM Council facilitated at once the post-activity compliance on the amendment and approval of LDRRM Plan 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit a justification on the aforementioned observation together with its references or results of market scanning conducted to avoid eventual disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: Management informed that before the issuance of Purchase Orders, prices were referred and compared with other nearby municipalities. All prices that were found to be higher than the once quoted, Management tried to have it negotiated with the suppliers to get the same price that is more advantageous to the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management prepare and submit the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances as of December 31, 2020 together with the necessary supporting documents and henceforth, adhere to the provisions and reportorial requirements set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020 to ensure that aids and donations received by the Municipality are properly accounted for.
observations.managementAction: Summary List of Donations Received and Distributed was already submitted as required in reply to the AOM issued. Concerned offices agreed to coordinate for proper reportorial compliance and adhere to the provisions to COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020 to ensure proper accountability of LGU on donations received.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management furnish the Barangays with adequate financial information to support receipt of the donated equipment and facilitate accurate recording thereof. Henceforth, we recommended that management adhere to the provisions of Sections 63 and 111 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Administrator met with the concerned Barangay Captains and their respective Barangay Treasurers and had them furnished with financial information. Such donations were properly acknowledged and recorded at barangay books for Year 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management determine and decide on the depreciation policies to be adopted pursuant to Government Accounting Manual for LGUs. In addition, we also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary journal entries to take up the depreciation expense and update the related accumulated depreciation accounts.
observations.managementAction: Needed adjustments and journal entries on the depreciation of Infrastructure Assets were done and reflected per Financial Reports dated April 30, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that all contracts entered by the government shall indicate all necessary information such as terms and conditions as set forth in Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management and other applicable procurement laws to avoid personal liability.
observations.managementAction: The management has already made necessary corrections and promised that these discrepancies be avoided if not totally eradicated in future transactions. This has been noted by BAC Secretariat and other procuring offices.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the contractor to immediately undertake the necessary measures to rectify the deficiencies in the project, otherwise, a refund of the equivalent amount should be collected from the contractor to avoid eventual disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The contractor immediately corrected the deficiency upon notification. The Municipal Engineer and the management itself will see to it that they will be more careful and keen in the assessment of all our future projects with regards to inspection of completed works, worthy of the cost of project being implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit a justification on the unnecessary disbursements to avoid eventual disallowance in audit. Henceforth, we recommended that management exercise prudence in the disbursement of government funds with the view of ensuring economy, efficiency and effectiveness in its operations.
observations.managementAction: Management justified that it was one way of promoting the tourism industry which was at its highest peak being the most visited place in the Province of Cebu not until the pandemic started last March 16, 2021. However, Management will do its best to always be prudent in managing and spending government funds giving priorities to programs most beneficial to all Santafehanons.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management submit the Environmental Clearance Certificate (ECC) pertinent to the above transaction for evaluation by this office, otherwise a notice of disallowance will be issued to this effect.

Henceforth, we recommended that the management ensure that all documentary requirements related to acquisition of real property are complied with pursuant to the provisions of RA 8974 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: RDY Constructor’s payment to secure Environment Clearance Certificate from DENR has been accepted already, of which copy of the Order of Payment and deposit made are attached. Management  will be sending immediately the copy of said document once they get a copy of said clearance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the municipal treasurer prepare and submit report on receivables to the municipal accountant as basis in the proper recording and monitoring of the receivables.

We also recommended that the municipal accountant record and reflect the necessary adjusting journal entry to recognize the amount of accumulated uncollected fees and to present fairly the financial statements in accordance with the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasury Office provided the Accounting Office with the list of receivables as of December 31, 2020 upon receipt of AOM and posted necessary journal entries to recognize the amount of accumulated uncollected fees in accordance with IPSAS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the management to secure a valid official receipt from the League of Municipalities in accordance with the National Internal Revenue Code and BIR regulations. 

Furthermore, we also recommended that the management submit justification and legal basis in providing financial assistance to the League of Municipalities.
observations.managementAction: LMP has already been informed of COA’s comments on their unregistered official receipts being issued to attending LGUs. Maybe by now, they could have already made their move for securing stubs of this particular document from BIR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management collect from the market stall occupants the corresponding deposits and advance payment otherwise impose sanctions for non-compliance thereof.
observations.managementAction: The management has already collected deposit and advance payments from market stalls’ occupants upon renewal of their contracts for 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management submit justification on why the procurement was awarded to a lone bidder despite having an expired Philgeps Certificate of Registration Blue Membership and not disqualified in the post-qualification to avoid disallowance.

We further recommended that the BAC and TWG exercise utmost prudence in the post-qualification of bids in order to properly determine whether the bidder concerned complies with and are responsive to all the requirements and conditions specified in accordance with the R.A. 9184 and its IRR and the GPPB Circular, otherwise, persons responsible should be held accountable.
observations.managementAction: Pristine Enterprise has completed the project. Management submitted photocopies of OR 2357617 dated July 9, 2020 for payment of said obligation and the demand letter duly received by supplier.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management make representation with the Department of Budget and Management (DBM) on the aforementioned deficiency and to expedite the establishment of its website to avoid non-compliance of similar requirement.
observations.managementAction: Application for LGU Website License has already been submitted and presently waiting for its approval. Said website is now existing but uploading data build-up of contents has still to be done.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management submit its basis on the actual rates used in the contract otherwise management consider amendments in the contract or on the municipal revenue code as applicable and ensure that the provisions and conditions are adequate in accordance with the prevailing local government policies.
observations.managementAction: The Local Finance Committee recommended the increase in rental, However, the same was put on hold until the  enactment of the Revised Revenue Code 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management submit its legal basis on the inclusion of lot acquisition under the build and design scheme for evaluation by this office and to appropriately make manifestation with the National Housing Authority on the noted deficiency in the aforementioned terms of reference.
observations.managementAction:
The inclusion of lot acquisition under the Build and Design scheme is premised on the crafted Terms of Reference(TOR) by the National Housing Authority for the procurement of fully developed serviced lots with completed housing units which may be undertaken and supported by Proclamation No. 682, issued on November 3, 2013 declaring a state of national calamity due to the widespread death and devastation caused by Super Typhoon Yolanda in the Provinces of Samar, Leyte, CEBU, Iloilo, Capiz, Aklan, Antique, Negros Occidental and Palawan. 
Thereafter, GPPB Resolution No. 34-2013 was issued on Nov.14, 2013, “granting all concerned procuring entities the authority to resort to negotiated procurement under 53.2(emergency cases) of the IRR of RA 9184, as amended. Further, as settled by the Government Corporate Counsel in its reply per letter dated July 22, 2014 to NHA, General Manager Atty.Chito M. Cruz, relating to NHA Letter of Inquiry dated June 24, 2014 relevant to “Contract Review” on the “Terms of Reference for the procurement of fully serviced lots with completed housing units”, the Government Corporate Counsel, Atty. Raoul Crencia, considered the crafted TOR of NHA as authorized and compliant with Section 53.2 of Revised IRR of RA 9184 under emergency cases and GPPB Resolution 34-2013 and render RA 10752 not applicable. Thus, the same is given due course.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management speed up the implementation of said projects and programs funded under the Bottom-Up Budgeting (BuB) and ensure that funds are optimally utilized with a view of ensuring efficiency, economy and effectiveness in the utilization of government resources for the benefit of its constituencies. Otherwise, the management shall return such fund to the source agency so that appropriate agencies could utilize the same for other equally important government projects.

We recommended that the management speed up the implementation of said projects and programs funded under the Bottom-Up Budgeting (BuB) and ensure that funds are optimally utilized with a view of ensuring efficiency, economy and effectiveness in the utilization of government resources for the benefit of its constituencies. Otherwise, the management should return such fund to the source agency so that appropriate agencies could utilize the same for other equally important government projects.
observations.managementAction: The retired Municipal Budget Officer has donated 400+ sq.m lot to Barangay Hilantagaan to include for Construction of Health Facility. With this update, BAC has again scheduled re-bidding of said project for a speedy and smooth implementation of this long-overdue project.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipal mayor direct the municipal planning and development officer and the GFPS to lead or spearhead the setting up and maintenance of a GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation in compliance with the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: The setting up of GAD Database has been stop since the LGU is still looking for competent IT expert to spearhead the completion and maintenance of said program.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the municipal treasurer and municipal accountant prepare reports on the operation and financial statements for the Fish Drying Facilities Project under the economic enterprises operation. We also recommended that the management maintain subsidiary ledger and submit the trial balance and financial statements pursuant to the Local Government Code and The Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for Local Government Units. Failure to submit the pertinent FS could be a ground for the withholding of salary.

We further recommended that the management come up with an operational guideline which will be used in the day to day operation of the Fish Drying Facilities Project.
observations.managementAction: Former Municipal Treasurer Ms. Palma E. Batobalonos who retired from service last November 26, 2021 has not received her Terminal Leave Pay for being unable to submit said financial statements. The management is still waiting for the submission of such.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management should limit the granting of additional benefits for personal services within the 55% level for a fourth class municipality and lower as provided for by law and to require all officials and employees to refund the amount of ₱10,000.00 each.

Furthermore, any review comments by the provincial finance committee on municipal budgets that needs to be complied with strictly must be taken into consideration before disbursing funds to avoid disallowance in post-audit.
observations.managementAction: Inspite of the absence of the issuance of ND, all outgoing regular employees have been deducted of ₱25,000 from their Terminal Leave Pays.

observations.recommendation:
Management should limit the grant of additional benefits for personal services within the 55% level for a fifth class municipality and lower as provided for by law and to require all officials and employees to refund the amount of ₱10,000.00 each.

Furthermore, any review comments by the provincial finance committee on municipal budgets that needs to be complied with strictly must be taken into consideration before disbursing funds to avoid disallowance in post-audit.
observations.managementAction: Inspite of the absence of the issuance of ND, all outgoing regular employees have been deducted of ₱25,000 from their Terminal Leave Pays.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipal treasurer provides complete and updated list of taxpayers with corresponding amount of real property tax due which shall be the basis of the municipal accountant in setting-up the actual RPT and SET receivables for the year pursuant to Section 20 of the NGAS Manual.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has contracted a 4M worth of Computerization Program for a systematic and efficient imposition of taxes to all property owners. The program is not fully utilized, and currently there is a staff from service provider who assisted the LGU in running the program. This would eventually solve the issue on the availability of updated lists of taxpayers with corresponding amount of RPT due which should be the basis of the Municipal Accountant in setting up receivables at the start of each calendar year.

observations.recommendation:
The ICO-Municipal Treasurer should be required to restitute immediately the missing funds.
We further recommended that the Barangay observes the provisions of Sections 123 and 124 of P.D. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Case has already been filed by Mr. Filomeno Luna of COA against Ms. Wilma Suico. No updates as of this date despite of follow up letters and resolutions submitted by Sannguniang Bayan on the matter few years ago.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Inventory Committee prepare and submit the reconciled Report of the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment for the past four (4) years in order to truly reflect the proper PPE total per year. Adjust the ledgers for validated discrepancies to be taken up in the Trial Balances for CY2021. Update and edit existing latest files in order to expedite the preparation of the RPCPPE. Exert effort and set aside time from regular functions to be spent for the preparation of the RPCPPE. Request for authority to render overtime to fulfill the preparation and submission of this salient municipal report as mentioned in Section 122 of PD 1445. The Inventory Committee should strategize plan how to tackle the preparation of the RPCPPE.  Conduct weekly monitoring of the accomplishments of the Inventory Committee in compliance with Section 266 of the GAM for LGUs. Discourage penalty and punishment for negligence in the performance of important duties to the government by complying with Section 266 of the GAM for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: No inventory Report of PPE was submitted by Management. However, they conducted physical inventory count in 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepares in full the necessary schedules required to support the existence and validity of real accounts in the Statement of Financial Position in compliance with PD No. 1445 and Chapter 2 the PPSAS to produce quality, transparent and accountable reports and information needed by all stakeholders.
observations.managementAction: Management fully complied to the audit recommendation/s.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management should declassify the semi-expendable items from the list of Property, Plant and Equipment in order to arrive at a fair valuation, proper presentation and classification of the PPE accounts  pursuant to the provisions of Section 5.4 of COA Circular 2016-006.
observations.managementAction: Management fully complied to the audit recommendation/s.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepares and submits to the Municipal Accountant the Schedule of Unreleased Checks every end of the year. The Municipal Accountant prepares and records the reversing entry in the books by a debit to Cash-in-Bank and a credit to Accounts Payable based on the Schedule submitted by the Treasurer. The Municipal Accountant traces the Unreleased Checks to the list of Outstanding Checks. The Municipal Accountants prepares the Bank Reconciliation Statement as a tool to determine the correctness of the Cash-in-Bank account. In this way, the proper balances of depository accounts are fairly presented in the financial statements in compliance with Section 352 of the GAM for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: Management fully complied to the audit recommendation/s.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management through the Municipal Accountant reverts back the CY2014 STF-LDRRMF amounting to P109,276.90 to the General Fund which shall be allocated for social services to benefit the Santa Feans in consonance with Section 3, Rule 18 of the IRR of RA 10121.
observations.managementAction: Management fully complied to the audit recommendation/s.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management refrain from capitalizing subsequent expenditures of government funds which are only intended for “repairs and maintenance” of the PPE in conformity with the provisions of JMC No. 2017-1 of the DILG and DBM and the requirements of IPSAS 17 in the recognition of costs to be added to the PPE. Workback to uncover the errors made in previous years, prepare the working paper to accumulate all errors made and prepare and record the necessary Journal entry to adjust the affected accounts. Management should return these misapplied funds to the DF. Further, segregate the annual program for capital outlays from repairs and maintenance costs for the WATSAN. With proper compliance, the Water Supply Systems account in the PPE and the Government Equity shall be properly valued and presented in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management fully complied to the audit recommendation/s.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant take up regular purchases of supplies and materials as Inventory accounts in the books and refrain from following erroneous misconceptions and notions on the recording of supplies and materials as outright expenses in compliance  with the provisions of Section 114 of the MNGAS. If ever purchases were recorded as outright expenses during the year, then the Accountant should prepare adjusting entry to take up the cost of unutilized supplies and materials still found in municipal offices at the end of the year as indicated in the Report of Physical Count of Inventories to comply with Section 124 of same Manual. In this manner, the true value of remaining unconsumed/ unused supplies at the end of the year is reported and Assets and Government Equity shall be fairly presented.
observations.managementAction: Management fully complied to the audit recommendation/s.
Management fully complied to the audit recommendation/s.
Management fully complied to the audit recommendation/s.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management should-
Classify the disallowances according to the addresses of liable persons to facilitate collection.
Serve Demand Letter and ask their commitment to pay by agreeing on the dates these payments shall be received by the Municipal Treasurer;
Pursue visitations until full liquidation is attained.
observations.managementAction: Settlement of disallowances was not yet made in full.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Liquidating Officer (1) refrain from maintaining cash reserves in the municipal coffers,  (2) deposit in full and intact all collections from the issuance of accountable forms, (3) the collectors remit daily and in full all collections of the Municipality, and (4) the Liquidating Officer request that the collectors prepare the deposit slip for their remittances by accounting thereon the denomination of the monies they are remitting in order to lessen the burden in the procedures of the LO in recounting the moneys remitted and preparing the accumulated deposit slip for the collections of the day.
observations.managementAction: Management fully complied to the audit recommendation/s.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management incorporate in the Schedule of Property, Plant and Equipment to support the PPE accounts in the Statement of Financial Position every end of the year the following information in order for the stakeholders to better understand the PPE accounts to prevent doubtful interpretation of figures, misunderstanding due to vagueness and poor quality of decisions, to wit:
monthly depreciation,
estimated useful life, 
accumulated depreciation, 
cost of impairment (if any),
carrying value, (cost less accumulated depreciation) 
condition whether serviceable, for repair, unserviceable, idle or for disposal,
in compliance with the requirements for PPE presentation as provided in IPSAS 17.
observations.managementAction: Disclosures on PPE not yet fully made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accountant prepares for which the MDRRMO submits to COA every 15th of the succeeding month the Report on the amounts received and used for disaster risk reduction and management activities of the Municipality in compliance with the reporting requirements of the MDRRMF in COA Circular 2012-002 to ensure full disclosure and transparency to the general public.
observations.managementAction: Management fully complied to the audit recommendation/s.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prepare and submit the necessary report on the Summary/ List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances to the NDRRMC and the COA for information and in compliance with COA Circular 2020-009 being a recipient of donations in-kind. Further, Management should secure, if possible, the actual acknowledgement receipt of recipients to show proof of the turnover of goods and to enhance transparency of the transactions that transpired. Management is also warned not to duplicate this same violation in the future in order that vital information during times of crisis is available to the stakeholders and to the public for validation and appreciation
observations.managementAction: Management fully complied to the audit recommendation/s.
Management was able to sell their unserviceable properties and had gained income from these sales. However, other unserviceable items of PPE subject for disposal not yet acted upon.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management obtain a website to which information on the utilization and extent of implementation of the PPAs from the fund of the Bayanihan Grant to Cities and Municipalities shall be posted. Perpetuate the website through continuous development, maintenance and subscription so that vital information and actions of the municipal officials and employees on the use of government funds shall be made transparent to regulatory bodies, concerned constituents, interested parties and other stakeholders. Much more, the municipal website could be a portal to disclose tourism attractions and activities that could earn revenues to the government coffers. Also, the Municipality should post in two more conspicuous places their BGCM fund reports.
observations.managementAction: Bayanihan Grant Reports were posted in the DILG website.
Management fully complied to the audit recommendation/s.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU refrain from entering into contract with business entities where officials and employees have direct and indirect pecuniary interests including their relatives up to the third civil degree by affinity/consanguinity in compliance with the IRR of RA No. 9184, RA No. 7160 and RA No. 6713.
observations.managementAction: Management fully complied to the audit recommendation/s.
Management fully complied to the audit recommendation/s.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to stop the practice of paying emergency expenses out of the cash advance of the Disbursing Officer which had been purposely withdrawn for the payment of wages, petty expenses and other maintenance and operating expenses. Management should apply for the bond of a special disbursing officer who shall request a cash advance subject to liquidation for emergency purposes just like the aforecited situations. Management is encouraged to comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Management fully complied to the audit recommendation/s.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality update the terms and conditions of the Loan Contract by amending Ordinance No. 054-2005 which should include the determination of the monetary liability and penalty of the Recipient for all acts of negligence to imbibe in the mind of the Recipient the gravity of responsibility and accountability for taking charge of government property. Much more, the Municipal Agriculture Office should perform regular monthly visitation/ inspection that could save the outcome of the Dispersal Program and prevent the recurrence of previous adverse events that may impair its success. Annual physical count should always be performed to update the inventory records due to granting of new dispersals, turnovers of custodianship and cancellations of accomplished contracts during the year. Stock ledger cards shall be maintained for each animal by the Municipal Accountant for control purposes. Exert all efforts to gain desirable management of the execution and outcome of the dispersal contracts  to prevent the loss of the scarce resources of the government.
observations.managementAction: Management fully complied to the audit recommendation/s.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to Management the following to improve the granting of financial assistance, donations and aids, to wit:
Indorse the indigent to the PLGU and other government agencies giving financial assistance to receive aid and authority for solicitation; 
Enact policy in the granting of donations based on classification, degree of  gravity and urgency of the situation and not merely on discretion in order to extend government funds to the needy and deserving constituents in an efficient manner. They should consider the short-term and long-term effects to the constituents which shall eliminate dependency on the Mayor’s funds considering that in granting financial assistance, he is not obliged to freely answer in full for the amount requested but should put in mind that he is accountable for these taxpayers’ funds which should be spent efficiently and effectively. 
Avoid duplication of giving assistance to a single person and same beneficiary  by maintaining an active database of the names of requesting persons and their beneficiaries at the office of the DSWD.
observations.managementAction: Management fully complied to the audit recommendation/s.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management and all persons involved prepare and submit their legal bases for the use of public funds particularly the purchase of a refrigerator costing P12,500.00 purposedly used as prize during a christmas party of the Department of Education, to be well evaluated and considered in audit if warranted by budgetary, accounting and auditing rules and regulations. However, if found unwarranted, Management and all persons involved should immediately return the amount of P12,500.00 for being disbursed without necessity and extravagantly and not within the bounds of the truthful mandates of the municipality. Management should only grant donations in the right sense such that donated funds should only be obligated and disbursed to address the necessary needs of indigent constituents. The In-Charge of District should exercise prudence  that she solicits expensive prizes from suppliers who, in the name of goodwill and business representation, can provide at their own personal expense  and not to be sourced from public funds of the municipality. Management is encouraged to refrain from defraying government funds for wasteful purposes and should abide by the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-003 and pertinent Sections of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Management fully complied to the audit recommendation/s.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality as member of the LMP -NV should join with other municipality members to craft the mechanisms for evaluation, monitoring and reporting of the implementation of the programs, projects and activities of all government funds received. Having a conflicting position being member contributors and implementors, the LMP -NV should refrain from receiving government funds from their respective agencies for implementation because these funds may be mismanaged and deviated to self-serving purposes due to conflicting interest.  The LMP -NV should refrain from the distribution of financial assistance to adversely affected constituents by calamities as this may spark issues of electioneering in view of the coming elections. The distribution of financial assistance by the LMP -NV is a duplication of the efforts of the municipality as mandated by the PPAs of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund and of the 20% Development Fund to address disasters. Instead, the LMP -NV should concentrate actively on  its primary purpose of ventilating, articulating and crystallizing issues affecting municipal government administration, and securing, through proper and legal means, solutions thereto as mandated by Section 496 of RA 7160. With these recommendations, the Management is also tasked to obtain the liquidation documents that reflect the utilization of municipal moneys conveyed to the LMP-NV for proper accountability reporting.
observations.managementAction: Management fully complied to the audit recommendation/s.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal social Welfare and Development officials involved in the processing, validation and distribution of Social Amelioration Program stipends immediately submit the applicable identification papers of all the beneficiaries who received the total of P13,006,800.00 which shall be used as corroborating papers to ascertain whether these actual beneficiaries were really qualified, eligible and entitled to the SAP stipends. Non-submission would constrain the COA to issue the Notice of Suspension (NS) to require these documents. Consequent non-settlement of this NS shall lead to the issuance of the Notice of Disallowance for SAP disbursements found irregular in audit.
observations.managementAction: Management fully complied to the audit recommendation/s.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management should always accomplish the documentary evidence of acknowledgement of recipients for items being distributed out of purchases using government funds. This is to prevent doubts formed  for failure of the auditor to ascertain the delivery and the utilization of purchased items.  Management is required to submit the acknowledgement receipt of frontliners for the PPEs and vitamins distributed to them, the schools and barangays  for the thermal scanners and the family/ household in the barangays for the relief goods received in compliance with the provisions of PD 1445, LBC No. 125 and COA Circular 2012-001. Failure to submit the required documents would constrain us to issue the Notice of Suspension (NS). Failure to settle the NS would lead to the issuance of a Notice of Disallowance for irregular transactions found in audit.
observations.managementAction: Management fully complied to the audit recommendation/s.
Management conducted physical taking of inventory and in the process of report preparation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Accountant derecognize the barangay properties funded out of municipal funds in favor of the barangays. The Journal Entry Voucher (JEV)  dropping these barangay accounts from the municipal books shall be copy furnished the barangay as the basis of the Barangay Bookkeeper of recognizing the actual cost of PPE to be recorded in the barangay ledgers. In this way, the municipal books shall only contain those PPEs which are being actually used by the municipality and which are in their custody to properly attain the fair valuation of municipal PPE and corresponding municipal investment in the Government Equity.
observations.managementAction: Management fully complied to the audit recommendation/s.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality should  always update the recording of the identified reconciling items in the Cash-in-Bank accounts which are derived from the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement as verified by the Auditor in order to arrive at the accurate valuation of maintained depository accounts which shall be used by Management in rendering the appropriate decision on the use of municipal funds.
observations.managementAction: Management fully complied to the audit recommendation/s.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management should-

(1) be aware of reconciling items appearing in the Bank Reconciliation Statements such which affect the presentation of the fair balance of the depository accounts. Much more in this case, Management should have-
 (2) noticed these reconciling items which had been dormant for years already and take action to address the situation. Management should-
(3) locate the payees of the stale checks especially the municipal employees and require them to file their request for check replacement afterwhich issue them their new checks to be acknowledged on a new disbursement voucher. In same manner, Management should-
(4) locate and properly identify the external creditors and issue them replacement checks to be acknowledged also on a new disbursement voucher. The non-presentation of the stale physical check in case it is inadvertently lost would not in any way harm the municipality because these could not be anymore negotiated for payment with the Bank and the creditor had already previously acknowledged payment when he issued the official receipt to the Municipality as attached to their disbursement vouchers while their acknowledgement on the new disbursement voucher would tantamount to their having received the new check payment after being properly identified as the legal and rightful claimant.
observations.managementAction: Stale checks still in the bank reconciliation statements and were not all acted upon.
Management fully complied to the audit recommendation/s.

Delinquencies or balance of RPT/SET Receivable at year end was not decreased. Collectibles for the period were collected and non-collection of previous years’ taxes might be due to the economic effects of the COVID19 Pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management enforce the liquidation of cash advances in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-004 and 97-002. Establish practicable mechanisms for the liquidation of all cash advances so that accumulation thereof in the books shall be prevented. It shall be the target of the Municipality to minimize the accumulation of balances by  the timely settlement through refund or the submission of liquidation documents within the prescribed period within which to liquidate them.
observations.managementAction: Cash advances for the current period were liquidated. However, balances of previous years still remained in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality take serious action to recover the funds for disallowed transactions which were disbursed through illegal means considering the materiality of accounts involved.  Exert extra and strategic efforts to initiate collection of these collectibles to return back to the government the funds which were illegally disbursed. This is a serious matter and undertaking considering the huge amount of money at stake if this is lost due to Management negligence and inaction. All it takes is a firm resolution to attain this government purpose which is a constant yearly recommendation from COA recorded in the annual audit reports. Besides, the collection of disallowances is a law of the land, a duty which needs to be fulfilled by government officials and employees.
observations.managementAction: Management fully complied to the audit recommendation/s.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management –

Firmly assume responsibility over the recovery of the monies disbursed over these Receivables, putting into the right perspective that the Municipality is a corporate entity with going-concern and thus, its operations, issues and concerns should be addressed by incumbent personalities forming the Management. Management should appoint the group of persons who shall be accountable and responsible for  every stage of the collection process who shall exert their efforts and their dedication for this cause. Prepare a monthly monitoring report per borrower of the demand letters issued and received by them, number of visitations and the commitment of borrower per visitation and the amounts collected to show the progress of the implementation of the audit recommendation. The honest efforts to fully comply with these audit recommendations shall all ultimately redound to the benefit of the Municipality as additional funds to supplement future budgets or funds available to be appropriated for additional priority PPAs.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted request for write off for eligible accounts.
Management did not implement in full the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality shall always be aware of its outstanding Payables at the end of each fiscal year and obligate/charge them against the current year appropriations and correspondingly set up the Accounts Payable account in compliance with regulations. In this way, the recording of expenses in the year these were incurred is properly accounted  and the Expenses relating to the year shall all be taken up and the Government Equity as at the end of the year shall also be fairly valued. Management shall be aware of its responsibility for the proper disposition of government funds under their custody.
observations.managementAction: Management implemented in full the audit recommendation.
Management responded to the requirements of the COVID1 Pandemic and this concern was fully implemented.

observations.recommendation: Based on the above premises, the Municipality is then asked to explain substantively the very reasons for granting the CNA despite not having met the minimum requirement for accomplishment for the granting of the CNA as at September 30, 2019 in accordance with DBM Circular No. 2019-5.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management send demand letters to these persons asking for the return of the amount due from them. Send two succeeding tracers to reinforce the intention of the Municipality to recover the funds. Strategize collection through the joint efforts of concerned municipal offices to properly return back government monies to the municipal coffers.  Otherwise, the balances shall be disallowed for lack of documents that shall warrant their liquidation considering the long period of time the funds had stayed out of government coffers and those who authorized their payments shall be solidarily liable with the payee-recipients.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

reorganize the Inventory Committee through the issuance of an Executive Order;
	
require the constituted Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of all Inventories and PPE of the Municipality and submit the corresponding reports to the Audit Team; and

thereafter, instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the resulting balance of the physical count with the accounting records in order to present the correct balances of said accounts in the financial statements.

In the event that deficiencies/discrepancies exist between the Inventory Report and accounting records, the necessary adjustments/corrections should be made.  Also, require the Inventory Committee to make a report of unserviceable/non-functional PPEs in the Inventory and Inspection Report on Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) which could be undertaken simultaneous with the physical inventory for disposal and eventual proper relief of accountabilities.

The actions taken by the Management do not correspond to partial compliance with the audit recommen-dations.  As of this reporting, the RPCI and RPCPPE for CY 2020 were not submitted to the Audit Team.

Local Chief Executive execute a new contract of lease with the seven stallholders whose lease contracts has already expired.
