observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Inventory Committee prepare and submit the reconciled Report of the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment for the past four (4) years in order to truly reflect the proper PPE total per year. Adjust the ledgers for validated discrepancies to be taken up in the Trial Balances for CY2021. Update and edit existing latest files in order to expedite the preparation of the RPCPPE. Exert effort and set aside time from regular functions to be spent for the preparation of the RPCPPE. Request for authority to render overtime to fulfill the preparation and submission of this salient municipal report as mentioned in Section 122 of PD 1445. The Inventory Committee should strategize plan how to tackle the preparation of the RPCPPE.  Conduct weekly monitoring of the accomplishments of the Inventory Committee in compliance with Section 266 of the GAM for LGUs. Discourage penalty and punishment for negligence in the performance of important duties to the government by complying with Section 266 of the GAM for LGUs.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepares in full the necessary schedules required to support the existence and validity of real accounts in the Statement of Financial Position in compliance with PD No. 1445 and Chapter 2 the PPSAS to produce quality, transparent and accountable reports and information needed by all stakeholders.

We recommended that Management should declassify the semi-expendable items from the list of Property, Plant and Equipment in order to arrive at a fair valuation, proper presentation and classification of the PPE accounts pursuant to the provisions of Section 5.4 of COA Circular 2016-006.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepares and submits to the Municipal Accountant the Schedule of Unreleased Checks every end of the year. The Municipal Accountant prepares and records the reversing entry in the books by a debit to Cash-in-Bank and a credit to Accounts Payable based on the Schedule submitted by the Treasurer. The Municipal Accountant traces the Unreleased Checks to the list of Outstanding Checks. The Municipal Accountants prepares the Bank Reconciliation Statement as a tool to determine the correctness of the Cash-in-Bank account. In this way, the proper balances of depository accounts are fairly presented in the financial statements in compliance with Section 352 of the GAM for LGUs.

We recommended that Management through the Municipal Accountant reverts back the CY2014 STF-LDRRMF amounting to P109,276.90 to the General Fund which shall be allocated for social services to benefit the Santa Feans in consonance with Section 3, Rule 18 of the IRR of RA 10121.

We recommended that Management refrain from capitalizing subsequent expenditures of government funds which are only intended for “repairs and maintenance” of the PPE in conformity with the provisions of JMC No. 2017-1 of the DILG and DBM and the requirements of IPSAS 17 in the recognition of costs to be added to the PPE. Workback to uncover the errors made in previous years, prepare the working paper to accumulate all errors made and prepare and record the necessary Journal entry to adjust the affected accounts. Management should return these misapplied funds to the DF. Further, segregate the annual program for capital outlays from repairs and maintenance costs for the WATSAN. With proper compliance, the Water Supply Systems account in the PPE and the Government Equity shall be properly valued and presented in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant take up regular purchases of supplies and materials as Inventory accounts in the books and refrain from following erroneous misconceptions and notions on the recording of supplies and materials as outright expenses in compliance with the provisions of Section 114 of the MNGAS. If ever purchases were recorded as outright expenses during the year, then the Accountant should prepare adjusting entry to take up the cost of unutilized supplies and materials still found in municipal offices at the end of the year as indicated in the Report of Physical Count of Inventories to comply with Section 124 of same Manual. In this manner, the true value of remaining unconsumed/ unused supplies at the end of the year is reported and Assets and Government Equity shall be fairly presented.

We recommended that Management should-
Classify the disallowances according to the addresses of liable persons to facilitate collection.
Serve Demand Letter and ask their commitment to pay by agreeing on the dates these payments shall be received by the Municipal Treasurer;
Pursue visitations until full liquidation is attained.

We recommended that the Liquidating Officer (1) refrain from maintaining cash reserves in the municipal coffers,  (2) deposit in full and intact all collections from the issuance of accountable forms, (3) the collectors remit daily and in full all collections of the Municipality, and (4) the Liquidating Officer request that the collectors prepare the deposit slip for their remittances by accounting thereon the denomination of the monies they are remitting in order to lessen the burden in the procedures of the LO in recounting the moneys remitted and preparing the accumulated deposit slip for the collections of the day.

We recommended that Management incorporate in the Schedule of Property, Plant and Equipment to support the PPE accounts in the Statement of Financial Position every end of the year the following information in order for the stakeholders to better understand the PPE accounts to prevent doubtful interpretation of figures, misunderstanding due to vagueness and poor quality of decisions, to wit:
monthly depreciation,
estimated useful life,
accumulated depreciation,
cost of impairment (if any),
carrying value, (cost less accumulated depreciation)
condition whether serviceable, for repair, unserviceable, idle or for disposal,
in compliance with the requirements for PPE presentation as provided in IPSAS 17.

We recommended that the Accountant prepares for which the MDRRMO submits to COA every 15th of the succeeding month the Report on the amounts received and used for disaster risk reduction and management activities of the Municipality in compliance with the reporting requirements of the MDRRMF in COA Circular 2012-002 to ensure full disclosure and transparency to the general public.

We recommended that Management prepare and submit the necessary report on the Summary/ List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances to the NDRRMC and the COA for information and in compliance with COA Circular 2020-009 being a recipient of donations in-kind. Further, Management should secure, if possible, the actual acknowledgement receipt of recipients to show proof of the turnover of goods and to enhance transparency of the transactions that transpired. Management is also warned not to duplicate this same violation in the future in order that vital information during times of crisis is available to the stakeholders and to the public for validation and appreciation

We recommended that Management obtain a website to which information on the utilization and extent of implementation of the PPAs from the fund of the Bayanihan Grant to Cities and Municipalities shall be posted. Perpetuate the website through continuous development, maintenance and subscription so that vital information and actions of the municipal officials and employees on the use of government funds shall be made transparent to regulatory bodies, concerned constituents, interested parties and other stakeholders. Much more, the municipal website could be a portal to disclose tourism attractions and activities that could earn revenues to the government coffers. Also, the Municipality should post in two more conspicuous places their BGCM fund reports.

We recommended that the LGU refrain from entering into contract with business entities where officials and employees have direct and indirect pecuniary interests including their relatives up to the third civil degree by affinity/consanguinity in compliance with the IRR of RA No. 9184, RA No. 7160 and RA No. 6713.

We recommended Management to stop the practice of paying emergency expenses out of the cash advance of the Disbursing Officer which had been purposely withdrawn for the payment of wages, petty expenses and other maintenance and operating expenses. Management should apply for the bond of a special disbursing officer who shall request a cash advance subject to liquidation for emergency purposes just like the aforecited situations. Management is encouraged to comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended that the Municipality update the terms and conditions of the Loan Contract by amending Ordinance No. 054-2005 which should include the determination of the monetary liability and penalty of the Recipient for all acts of negligence to imbibe in the mind of the Recipient the gravity of responsibility and accountability for taking charge of government property. Much more, the Municipal Agriculture Office should perform regular monthly visitation/ inspection that could save the outcome of the Dispersal Program and prevent the recurrence of previous adverse events that may impair its success. Annual physical count should always be performed to update the inventory records due to granting of new dispersals, turnovers of custodianship and cancellations of accomplished contracts during the year. Stock ledger cards shall be maintained for each animal by the Municipal Accountant for control purposes. Exert all efforts to gain desirable management of the execution and outcome of the dispersal contracts to prevent the loss of the scarce resources of the government.

We recommended to Management the following to improve the granting of financial assistance, donations and aids, to wit:
Indorse the indigent to the PLGU and other government agencies giving financial assistance to receive aid and authority for solicitation;
Enact policy in the granting of donations based on classification, degree of gravity and urgency of the situation and not merely on discretion in order to extend government funds to the needy and deserving constituents in an efficient manner. They should consider the short-term and long-term effects to the constituents which shall eliminate dependency on the Mayor’s funds considering that in granting financial assistance, he is not obliged to freely answer in full for the amount requested but should put in mind that he is accountable for these taxpayers’ funds which should be spent efficiently and effectively.
Avoid duplication of giving assistance to a single person and same beneficiary by maintaining an active database of the names of requesting persons and their beneficiaries at the office of the DSWD.

We recommended that Management and all persons involved prepare and submit their legal bases for the use of public funds particularly the purchase of a refrigerator costing P12,500.00 purposely used as prize during a Christmas party of the Department of Education, to be well evaluated and considered in audit if warranted by budgetary, accounting and auditing rules and regulations. However, if found unwarranted, Management and all persons involved should immediately return the amount of P12,500.00 for being disbursed without necessity and extravagantly and not within the bounds of the truthful mandates of the municipality. Management should only grant donations in the right sense such that donated funds should only be obligated and disbursed to address the necessary needs of indigent constituents. The In-Charge of District should exercise prudence that she solicits expensive prizes from suppliers who, in the name of goodwill and business representation, can provide at their own personal expense and not to be sourced from public funds of the municipality. Management is encouraged to refrain from defraying government funds for wasteful purposes and should abide by the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-003 and pertinent Sections of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipality as member of the LMP -NV should join with other municipality members to craft the mechanisms for evaluation, monitoring and reporting of the implementation of the programs, projects and activities of all government funds received. Having a conflicting position being member contributors and implementors, the LMP -NV should refrain from receiving government funds from their respective agencies for implementation because these funds may be mismanaged and deviated to self-serving purposes due to conflicting interest. The LMP -NV should refrain from the distribution of financial assistance to adversely affected constituents by calamities as this may spark issues of electioneering in view of the coming elections. The distribution of financial assistance by the LMP -NV is a duplication of the efforts of the municipality as mandated by the PPAs of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund and of the 20% Development Fund to address disasters. Instead, the LMP -NV should concentrate actively on its primary purpose of ventilating, articulating and crystallizing issues affecting municipal government administration, and securing, through proper and legal means, solutions thereto as mandated by Section 496 of RA 7160. With these recommendations, the Management is also tasked to obtain the liquidation documents that reflect the utilization of municipal moneys conveyed to the LMP-NV for proper accountability reporting.

We recommend that the Municipality provide competent legal basis specifically authorizing the granting of the Economic Resiliency Assistance (ERA) to LGUs, otherwise, the same shall be disallowed and returned back to the government coffers for having been disbursed in violation of government laws, rules and regulations.  The Municipality shall only grant additional personnel benefits as provided and mandated by government rules and regulations and by competent authority. The Municipality is also advised to refrain from duplicating the granting of benefit of same nature in future operations.

We recommend that the Municipal social Welfare and Development officials involved in the processing, validation and distribution of Social Amelioration Program stipends immediately submit the applicable identification papers of all the beneficiaries who received the total of P13,006,800.00 which shall be used as corroborating papers to ascertain whether these actual beneficiaries were really qualified, eligible and entitled to the SAP stipends. Non-submission would constrain the COA to issue the Notice of Suspension (NS) to require these documents. Consequent non-settlement of this NS shall lead to the issuance of the Notice of Disallowance for SAP disbursements found irregular in audit.

We recommended that Management should always accomplish the documentary evidence of acknowledgement of recipients for items being distributed out of purchases using government funds. This is to prevent doubts formed for failure of the auditor to ascertain the delivery and the utilization of purchased items.  Management is required to submit the acknowledgement receipt of frontliners for the PPEs and vitamins distributed to them, the schools and barangays for the thermal scanners and the family/ household in the barangays for the relief goods received in compliance with the provisions of PD 1445, LBC No. 125 and COA Circular 2012-001. Failure to submit the required documents would constrain us to issue the Notice of Suspension (NS). Failure to settle the NS would lead to the issuance of a Notice of Disallowance for irregular transactions found in audit.

We recommended that Management, through its Inventory Committee, conduct physical count of inventories and properties of the Municipality, reconcile the results of the count with the accounting records and submit the complete and accurate RPCI and RPCPPE to the Audit Team, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1.

On the procurement aspect, we recommended that the BAC strictly comply with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 in the conduct of procurement activities on the projects of the Municipality.

Moreover, we recommended that the BAC carefully prepare the needed documents/reports in order to provide complete, correct and consistent information to the users thereof.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended that the:
Members of the GFPS (i) properly classify the PPAs into mandated and attributed programs in the GPB depending on the source of funds thereof.  If the programs are identified PPAs of other offices, classify those as attributed programs and administer the HGDG tests in order to determine the portion of the budgets that may be attributed to GAD.  Proper classification of the PPAs in the GPB is necessary in arriving at the correct appropriations for GAD Fund; (ii) see to it that the gender issues are properly indicated in the project briefs/proposals of the identified GAD PPAs to be able to validate its inclusion in the GPB aside from results of the HGDG tests; (iii) ensure the correctness of the computation of the scores provided in the checklists to arrive at the correct amount of the budgets that may be attributed to GAD; (iv) submit explanation on the non-inclusion of the projects – Establishment of Fitness and Wellness Facility (Phase II), Establishment of Handwashing Facility and Sustainable Sanitation Program in the AIP; (v) ensure that the GAD Fund are properly utilized particularly on the conduct of GAD-related activities which involves procurement of catering services; (vi) thoroughly check the completeness of the attachments of GAD AR; and (vii) carefully prepare the Narrative ARs to be able to provide accurate and reliable information to the users thereof.  In doing this, see to it that consistent information are contained in the Narrative AR and other documents  attached in the disbursements of the GAD PPAs;
BAC see to it that the POs and their supporting documents are submitted to the Audit Team within the period prescribed in COA Circular No. 2009-001; and
Municipal Accountant ensure the correctness of the disbursements accounted in the List of Vouchers/Projects Charged Against the Fund since this will be used as the basis in the actual expenditures that will be forwarded in the GAD AR.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) comply
with the mandatory posting of the updated APP, NOA, and other information
relative to the Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act in the GPPB
Online Portal as required in GPPB Circular 01-2020. Henceforth, we also
recommended that the BAC continuously update themselves with the issuances
and advisories of the GPPB and other related regulatory agencies on the
conduct of procurement activities to avoid non-compliance thereof.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit a
justification for the above noted deficiencies and ensure that the OSS are
notarized accordingly. Henceforth, we recommended that the BAC continuously
update themselves with the issuances and advisories of the GPPB and other
related regulatory agencies on the conduct of procurement activities to avoid
non-compliance thereof.

We recommended that management observe the procedural rules and the
proper classifications in the accounting and recording of its supplies and
properties in accordance with the New Government Accounting System and
Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSASs) and other similar
issuances to avoid financial misstatements.

We recommended that Management make representation with the
Department of Budget and Management (DBM) on the aforementioned
deficiency and to expedite the establishment of its website to avoid non-
compliance of similar requirement.

We recommended that the responsible officials submit a justification on
the noted deficiency and the inclusion of ineligible beneficiaries as well as the
lacking documentary requirements. If warranted, demand for a refund for funds
illegally received and/or impose corresponding sanctions as provided for in
Memorandum Circular No. 09 Series of 2020 dated April 9, 2020.

We recommended that Management prepare the supplemental
LDRRMFIP reflecting the changes thereto and effect its approval in accordance
with the provisions set forth in Item 5.1.3.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and
Item 2.0 of DILG DBM JMC No. 02.

We recommended that Management submit a justification on the
aforementioned observation together with its references or results of market
scanning conducted to avoid eventual disallowance in audit.

We recommended that management prepare and submit the
Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances as of December
31, 2020 together with the necessary supporting documents and henceforth,
adhere to the provisions and reportorial requirements set forth in COA Circular
No. 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020 to ensure that aids and donations received by
the Municipality are properly accounted for.

We recommended that Management furnish the Barangays with
adequate financial information to support receipt of the donated equipment and
facilitate accurate recording thereof. Henceforth, we recommended that
management adhere to the provisions of Sections 63 and 111 of Presidential
Decree No. 1445.

We recommended that the Management determine and decide on the
depreciation policies to be adopted pursuant to Government Accounting Manual
for LGUs. In addition, we also recommended that the Municipal Accountant
prepare the necessary journal entries to take up the depreciation expense and
update the related accumulated depreciation accounts.

We recommended that Management ensure that all contracts entered by
the government shall indicate all necessary information such as terms and
conditions as set forth in Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property
Management and other applicable procurement laws to avoid personal liability.

We recommended that Management require the contractor to
immediately undertake the necessary measures to rectify the deficiencies in the
project, otherwise, a refund of the equivalent amount should be collected from
the contractor to avoid eventual disallowance in audit.

We recommended that management submit a justification on the
unnecessary disbursements to avoid eventual disallowance in audit. Henceforth,
we recommended that management exercise prudence in the disbursement of
government funds with the view of ensuring economy, efficiency and
effectiveness in its operations.

observations.managementAction: Management conducted physical taking of inventory and in the process of report preparation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Accountant derecognize the barangay properties funded out of municipal funds in favor of the barangays. The Journal Entry Voucher (JEV)  dropping these barangay accounts from the municipal books shall be copy furnished the barangay as the basis of the Barangay Bookkeeper of recognizing the actual cost of PPE to be recorded in the barangay ledgers. In this way, the municipal books shall only contain those PPEs which are being actually used by the municipality and which are in their custody to properly attain the fair valuation of municipal PPE and corresponding municipal investment in the Government Equity.
observations.managementAction: Management partially implemented the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality should  always update the recording of the identified reconciling items in the Cash-in-Bank accounts which are derived from the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement as verified by the Auditor in order to arrive at the accurate valuation of maintained depository accounts which shall be used by Management in rendering the appropriate decision on the use of municipal funds.
observations.managementAction: Management made partial implementation of the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management should-

(1) be aware of reconciling items appearing in the Bank Reconciliation Statements such which affect the presentation of the fair balance of the depository accounts. Much more in this case, Management should have-
 (2) noticed these reconciling items which had been dormant for years already and take action to address the situation. Management should-
(3) locate the payees of the stale checks especially the municipal employees and require them to file their request for check replacement afterwhich issue them their new checks to be acknowledged on a new disbursement voucher. In same manner, Management should-
(4) locate and properly identify the external creditors and issue them replacement checks to be acknowledged also on a new disbursement voucher. The non-presentation of the stale physical check in case it is inadvertently lost would not in any way harm the municipality because these could not be anymore negotiated for payment with the Bank and the creditor had already previously acknowledged payment when he issued the official receipt to the Municipality as attached to their disbursement vouchers while their acknowledgement on the new disbursement voucher would tantamount to their having received the new check payment after being properly identified as the legal and rightful claimant.
observations.managementAction: Stale checks still in the bank reconciliation statements and were not all acted upon.
Same finding made on the Liquidating Officer and Collecting Officers during cash examination but could be due to the practice of Alternative Work Arrangement during COVID19 Pandemic.

Delinquencies or balance of RPT/SET Receivable at year end was not decreased. Collectibles for the period were collected and non-collection of previous years’ taxes might be due to the economic effects of the COVID19 Pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Abstract of Real Property Collections form at the end of each month in compliance with Sections 20 and 34 of the MNGAS and copy furnish the Provincial Accountant as prescribed for reconciliation purposes.
observations.managementAction: Management implemented the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management enforce the liquidation of cash advances in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-004 and 97-002. Establish practicable mechanisms for the liquidation of all cash advances so that accumulation thereof in the books shall be prevented. It shall be the target of the Municipality to minimize the accumulation of balances by  the timely settlement through refund or the submission of liquidation documents within the prescribed period within which to liquidate them.
observations.managementAction: Cash advances for the current period were liquidated. However, balances of previous years still remained in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality take serious action to recover the funds for disallowed transactions which were disbursed through illegal means considering the materiality of accounts involved.  Exert extra and strategic efforts to initiate collection of these collectibles to return back to the government the funds which were illegally disbursed. This is a serious matter and undertaking considering the huge amount of money at stake if this is lost due to Management negligence and inaction. All it takes is a firm resolution to attain this government purpose which is a constant yearly recommendation from COA recorded in the annual audit reports. Besides, the collection of disallowances is a law of the land, a duty which needs to be fulfilled by government officials and employees.
observations.managementAction: Prior year’s disallowed granting of the SSL was almost collected while disallowances in FY2009 to FY2016 had just minimal collection.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality must be the catalyst for barangay development through the proper monitoring of the implementation of barangay projects according to their planned stages of completion in order to ensure that the benefit of economic progress shall address the needs of the people in the grassroots in a timely and efficient manner.
observations.managementAction: The current year transfers were outstanding. Previous year’s transfers were already liquidated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management –
Always require contractors to post their Performance Bond before the signing of the Contract with the Municipality; 
Require the affected Contractors to post their Performance Bonds on unfinished projects as at year end; and
Always attach a copy of the Performance Security to the disbursement voucher as a reference to the faithful performance of the obligation of the Contractor.
observations.managementAction: Management implemented the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management should require all affected Contractors to post additional Surety Bonds in the total amount of P5,657,160.19 for submission to the Municipality for their completed projects in order to protect the best interest of the Municipality of its completed infrastructure projects that is to obtain assurance that sufficient funds are available to finance the repairs of project structural defects that might occur before the final acceptance of the projects within one year after the projects were completed.
observations.managementAction: Management implemented the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer reconcile their records on the Appropriations by the Budget Officer for the purchase and acquisition of PPE and the actual PPE expenditures based on the ledgers of the Municipal Accountant. In this way, the correct amount of Capital Outlay as budgeted and expended are reconciled and the disposition of funds for capital expenditures are properly accounted with.
observations.managementAction: Management implemented the audit recommendation.
Management did not implement in full the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality shall always be aware of its outstanding Payables at the end of each fiscal year and obligate/charge them against the current year appropriations and correspondingly set up the Accounts Payable account in compliance with regulations. In this way, the recording of expenses in the year these were incurred is properly accounted  and the Expenses relating to the year shall all be taken up and the Government Equity as at the end of the year shall also be fairly valued. Management shall be aware of its responsibility for the proper disposition of government funds under their custody.
observations.managementAction: Management did not implement in full the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that to prevent the non-submission of required documents, the Accountant should attach a list of all required supporting documents per transaction to all vouchers presented in her office for processing, validation and certification for all claimants to comply with. In this way, requirements are submitted as listed to hasten the processing of claims and prevent delay in document review and processing when claims are not attended to because the validity of amounts due could not be established on insufficient attachments.
observations.managementAction: Management implemented the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management particularly the Bids and Awards Committee (1) carefully prepare, (2) fully accomplish and (3) completely submit all necessary documents to bidding procurements for infrastructure projects for downloaded funds in order to support their validity, legality and propriety to avoid receiving Notice of Suspension requiring the submission of lacking documents or Notice of Disallowance for the return of monies for transacting illegal, invalid and irregular disbursements of government funds.
observations.managementAction: Management implemented the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record in the General Fund as Income all collections from the PCSO for the municipal STL monthly shares.
observations.managementAction: Management implemented the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer exert its efforts to recover copies of the market stalls lease agreements from the occupants. See to it that the documents are valid and devoid of defects so that Management can enforce fully the obligations of the stall holders in case of default in payment of dues, destruction of leased properties, violation of obligated duties and responsibilities and other instances which might harm or impair the rights and interests of the Municipal Government over the market and its productive operations.
observations.managementAction: Management implemented the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management –
Identify properly the PPAs that needed implementation in the environment and business sectors, whether these are still applicable and need prioritization. If not, reprogram the funds to more pressing priority projects. 
Fast track the implementation of these priority projects through proper project identification, planning of the implementation scheme and resource management such as equipment, personnel and materials considering the optimization of weather conditions which particularly affect the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Minimal balance of allotments remained. Implementation was constrained by the COVID19 Pandemic.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that for the unimplemented Capital Outlay funds, Management should-

properly identify the PPAs that shall be accustomed to the actual needs of the times in order to properly respond to calamities and match the necessities that strengthen the capability of the LGU including the barangay level having the municipality as the core initiator, coordinator and implementer of calamity related PPAs of disaster risk reduction such as prevention, mitigation, preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery.
observations.managementAction: Management implemented the audit recommendations and implemented DRRM PPAs in response to the COVID19 Pandemic.
Management responded to the requirements of the COVID1 Pandemic and this concern was not fully implemented.
Management implemented the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: Based on the above premises, the Municipality is then asked to explain substantively the very reasons for granting the CNA despite not having met the minimum requirement for accomplishment for the granting of the CNA as at September 30, 2019 in accordance with DBM Circular No. 2019-5.
observations.managementAction: Awaiting response on query addressed to the DBM.
A significant number of vehicles especially those serviceable and used in operations were registered and secured with insurance with the GSIS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant requests the monthly updated status of loan accounts of borrower employees so that she is able to determine the sufficiency of amounts to be withheld from their salaries to satisfy the required monthly amortizations of their loans in order to help the borrower member avoid delinquency by withholding the proper amount for remittance to the GSIS. This action shall also assist the borrower maintain his good credit standing considering that information on their repayment performance on their GSIS loans are being provided to the Credit Information Corporation (CIC) as required by Republic Act No. 9510 also known as the Credit Information System Act, the repository of credit information from which credit checking and investigation could be accessed by all creditors whether public or private.
observations.managementAction: Management implemented the recommendation. The Auditor did not receive any adverse notification from the GSIS on employee loan repayments.

observations.recommendation:
The municipality is recommended to charge to the 20% Development Fund only those expenses which were disbursed for capital expenditures and contribute to the creation of tangible physical existence of the investments allowed in the Fund. Charging against the Fund of wages of laborers and contractual personnel not contributing to the creation/addition to capital assets/investments is discouraged. Consider adding to the capital asset accounts those investment expenditures which are aligned to the Fund purposes. Rectify and adjust the affected asset accounts to reflect their correct values/costs in the succeeding financial statements of the Municipality.

On the cost of labor which are spent for the fabrication/production of capital assets for the Forest Park economic enterprises and WATSAN, we recommended that separate program of works to show the estimates for materials and labor components is needed from which to base all capital charges. Such program of works shall be attached to all the corresponding vouchers to signal that these claims need to be capitalized under the 20 DF%. 

The Municipality is also encouraged to separate from the 20% DF all non-capital outlay components and charge them from the corresponding office appropriations in the General Fund. 

We also recommended that Management return to the 20%DF the misused monies and reprogram them for other priority development projects as identified by the Local Development Council. The status of implementation of these recommendations shall be reported and monitored in succeeding audit periods.
observations.managementAction: Management has refrained from using the 20% DF for the purchase of materials not capital outlay in nature. However, labor cost of employees of the WATSAN were still paid from the DF.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the LGU augment their pool of heavy equipment to address the need for readiness at all times in order to simultaneously implement projects and maximize manpower and utilization of equipment during good weather. Procurements through public bidding must be properly planned and conducted simultaneously to give opportunity to various suppliers and contractors to participate in the provision of materials and services for municipal projects. Augment also the personnel in the Engineering Office to attend to the preparation of program/project estimates and expedite the preparation of procurement documents. Additional personnel should also be hired to monitor project implementation to ensure that projects are implemented according to specifications to attain their desired and optimum quality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has not implemented P6.1M out of its P28.2M current appropriations/allotments. This is attributed to the constraints imposed by the COVID19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the privileges of men shall also be given priority and allocation of funds in succeeding annual GAD plans in compliance with the issued JMCs.
observations.managementAction: The recommendation was partially implemented in FY2020 constrained by the Covid19 Pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accountant prioritize the reconciliation of the Due to National Government subsidiary ledgers with the Trial Balance as at year-end to establish proper ending balances to be forwarded in the ensuing year. Moreover, Management shall obtain the proper information and continue implementing the projects in accordance with funds still available.
observations.managementAction: The Accountant is still in the process of reconciling Trust Liabilities due to the national government agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment by the designated Property Inventory Committee by indicating also therein the cost and the persons in charge and their respective offices to pinpoint accountability;  reconcile it with the accounting records;  book-up donated PPE in accordance to the Memorandum of Agreement or Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment, as the case may be which was executed  by the donor agency;  segregate unserviceable items beyond repair and report them in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) so that the PPE accounts are properly accounted in the financial statements and unserviceable items are disposed within the limits of authority; and identify lost items and request for relief from accountability.
observations.managementAction: No inventory of PPE was submitted as of the end of the audit fieldwork but physical count was made by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that as at the end of the calendar year, the Accountant should return back to the Cash-in-Bank balance the total amount of unreleased checks to reflect the accurate and proper amount of depository accounts in the financial statements. The Treasurer should give the list of unreleased checks to the Accountant for proper recording.
observations.managementAction: The book balances were understated due to unrecognized unreleased checks at the end of FY2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit intact his collections in the General Fund as at year-end especially on the last transaction day of every fiscal year in order to safekeep municipal funds at the depository bank and prevent the possibility of loss or misuse of scarce resources. On the stale checks, the Accountant should adjust the amount to Cash-in-Bank instead of to Cash-in-Local Treasury to return back the deducted amount of checks issued but should be returned back to the bank balance because of their being estopped from being transacted.
observations.managementAction: Undeposited collection balance of P93,943.61 as at year end was not maintained at minimal level.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accountant always attach adequate if not complete supporting schedules to real accounts in the year-end FS to fully explain what these accounts are made of in compliance with government reporting requirements in Section 111 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: The FS at year end still lack necessary schedules but the Accountant had been working partially on important accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that for the ensuing year, Management shall be vigilant and put up a plan of procurement so that the unimplemented projects as at the end of the year shall be purchased/constructed in 2019 in order that the people enjoy the benefits of protection in preparation of and from the aftermaths of disasters.
observations.managementAction: DRRM PPAs were maximized due to the requirements of the COVID19 Pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that as at the end of the year, all expenses of the Municipality shall be booked and recognized even the petty operating expenses spent from the Petty Cash Fund in order to present properly the correct amounts of Expense accounts and the resulting Government Equity which is decreased in case of net loss and increased in case of net income from operations.
observations.managementAction: Minimal balance of PCF of P148.00 was not returned as at year end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management enforce the liquidation of cash advances in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 as stated above.
observations.managementAction: Reduction to P195,599.68  was made to the accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accountant prioritize the reconciliation of the Due to LGUs subsidiary ledgers with the Trial Balance as at year-end to establish proper ending balances to be forwarded in the ensuing year. Moreover, Management shall obtain the proper information and continue implementing the projects in accordance with funds still available. The barangay with negative balances shall earn in their accounts amount that offset the overdraft and return the funds to the proper claimants.
observations.managementAction: Still in reconciliation process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile these two accounts by going back to the previous transactions, analyzing what should be made and preparing the JEV to adjust the accounts to reflect the proper real account balances.
observations.managementAction: Discrepancy was not yet reconciled and zeroed out.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality take serious action to recover the funds for disallowed transactions through legal means if necessary considering the materiality of accounts involved and the corresponding costs of collecting them. Exert extra and strategic efforts to initiate collection of these collectibles to return back to the government the funds which were illegally disbursed.
observations.managementAction: Significant decrease in collectibles was made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accountant reconcile the RPT and SET Receivables and related Deferred Tax accounts per year to facilitate comparison of data; Adjust the affected accounts whenever necessary in order to depict the true value of collectible real property taxes as at year end.
observations.managementAction: Discrepancy still exists in the accounts due to the provincial collection of SEF which when remitted to the MLGU were not paired with the other 1% tax collection for the General Fund which is a wrong treatment. Still in the reconciliation process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality provide legal basis of granting the CREA otherwise, same shall be returned back to the government coffers for having been disbursed in violation of government laws, rules and regulations.  The Municipality is also advised to refrain from duplicating the granting of benefit of same nature in future operations.
observations.managementAction: Disbursements for the Economic Resiliency Assistance was made. The COA inquired with the DBM on the legality of the payment.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated that Sections 63 and 68 of PD No. 1445 be implemented to establish proper accountability for collections which could be made anytime whenever audit procedures require. The practice therefore of issuing official receipts immediately for all collections shall be observed. Regarding the collections at the Balete Pass, cash tickets in denominations of P5, P10 and P20 which may be acquired from the National Printing Office could be utilized instead of Accountable Form 51 which  may be issued for greater amounts such as rentals of picnic huts, nipa huts, cottages and the halls. All receipts including unused cash tickets for the day should be accounted with the beginning cash tickets held and the received cash tickets for use to arrive at the total collections which may be remitted daily or as agreed upon for convenience of both the employee and the Municipal Treasurer considering that there are also lean days during the week.

On the sales of the OTOP Store, a regular inventory taking of twice a month may be observed to report items sold from beginning inventory and purchases after an ending inventory is established from physical count. A certain profit percentage on cost should be established per product classification should also be observed to facilitate the computation of sales. Inventory items should be grouped according to nature to facilitate inventory taking and reporting. The Accountable Form No.51 should be issued for the sales at the OTOP Store which should be remitted regularly which can be done once a day. Inventory items shall be encoded in forms which shall be used to account for the quantity at the beginning, quantity received, quantity of inventory end to arrive at the quantity sold.

We also observed that sales of the OTOP store were recorded as  a trust receipt which was rolled over for purchases. Since it is an enterprise in the LGU with grants already received for its proper establishment, the sales of the OTOP Store should be properly accounted as income generated in the General Fund which means that if income increase, then the basis for the limitation of Personnel Services also increase, thus is beneficial for the increase in salaries of the municipal officials and employees 

With a new way of handling and managing cash in the economic enterprise, the Municipality is assured of performing better commensurate with the services they render to the people.
observations.managementAction: Significant improvements were made with the collection and disbursement systems for the MTPDO.

In CY 2020, the projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended balances of LDRRMF of previous years were already disclosed in a separate Plan.  The MDRRMO also properly classified the PPAs as to MOOE and CO in the Plan.  He likewise ensured that all the disbursements paid out of the Fund are connected with disaster risk reduction and management activities.  Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant already set-up an entry amounting to P26,208.08 in August 2020 pertaining to the transactions in CY 2019 which should  not have been charged in the Fund.  On the disbursements totaling P285,970.00 in CY 2019  which should not have been capitalized, the necessary adjusting entries  were already made by the Municipal Accountant in August 2020.  It was also observed that the MDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant ensured that all the disbursements were accounted in the documents/reports.
observations.reason: The MDRRMO still failed to incorporate the information on continuing projects in the LDRRMFIP for CY 2020.   Furthermore, the Municipal Engineer and the MPDC still failed to submit written explanation on the photographs submitted relative to the payment of wages of laborers for the project – Furnishing and Improvement of Disaster Operation Center funded from the LDRRMF for CY 2017.  Also, the Audit Team still observed some instances when copies of POs and their supporting documents were submitted beyond the period prescribed by COA Circular No. 2009-001.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Development Council always ensure that the appropriations provided to support its development projects are sufficient as required by the existing laws, rules and regulations.
	
Moreover, we recommended that the MPDC and the Municipal Engineer: 

strictly comply with Items 2.0 and 3.0 of the JMC in the identification of projects to be charged against the 20% DF; 

carefully plan the imple-mentation of the projects and effectively execute the construction schedule to be able to timely deliver the services to the intended beneficiaries thereof; and

submit additional photographs to justify the final payment made to the laborers relative to the project – Rehabilitation of Guintigbasan Multi-Purpose Hall. 

We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer properly supervise the implementation of the projects which includes ensuring that the laborers who were hired for the actual construction thereof strictly follow the period of employment indicated in the respective Job Orders. 

The Municipal Accountant is also recommended to thoroughly check the completeness of the supporting documents attached in the claims.

We likewise recommended that the MPDC, Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant jointly coordinate in the preparation of reports to be able to present complete and reliable information to the users thereof.
observations.managementAction: During CY 2020, the Municipality has properly appropriated an amount of P15,749,065.40 to finance its priority development projects.  Furthermore, all the projects identified in the AIP were in accordance with Item 3.0 of the JMC.  Out of the total appropriations amounting to P15,749,065.40, projects with appropriations totaling P4,500,000.00 equivalent to 28.57% were not implemented during CY 2020.  This only showed that the Municipality see to it that majority of the PPAs charged against the 20% DF for CY 2020 were implemented during the year.  Also, verification of the supporting documents on the payments of wages of laborers on the projects implemented by administration in CY 2020 showed that the Municipal Engineer ensured that the laborers who were hired were indicated in the respective Job Orders.  He also see to it that no laborers were paid twice on a particular day for the same project.
observations.reason: The Municipality still failed to implement various projects sourced out from continuing appropriations as of December 31, 2020.  Incomplete and inconsistent information contained in the List of Vouchers/Projects Charged Against the Fund and the Report on PPAs for CY 2020 were still observed.  The lacking photographs for the implementation of the projects – Rehabilitation of Municipal Roads (Poblacion) and Rehabilitation of Guintigbasan Multi-Purpose Hall in CY 2019 were not yet submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.managementAction: The BAC has already addressed some of the observations in the procurement of infrastructure projects undertaken in CY 2020.
observations.reason: There are still lapses noted in the procurement process during the auditorial review of the contract and its supporting documents in CY 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant facilitate the submission of the aforementioned lacking documents to the Audit Team.  Moreover, we recommended that they should check the completeness and correctness of the supporting documents attached to the claims for payment of infrastructure projects in compliance with the provisions cited in COA Circular No. 2012-001.  If upon evaluation thereof, some of the information contained in some documents are inconsistent and/or the supporting documents are incomplete, require the concerned contractor to clarify the deficiencies noted and the submission of lacking documents before making payments thereto. 

We further recommended that the Municipal Engineer, being the main overseer of the implementation of projects, should also ensure that the information to be presented in the documents/reports to be issued by the Municipality are consistent.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer see to it that the information presented in the documents/ reports issued by the Municipality are consistent.   Furthermore, substantial compliance with the documentary requirements enumerated in COA Circular No. 2012-001 was observed on the payments made to the contractor on the project – Local Access Road in Barangay Poblacion A. Medina Street during CY 2020.
observations.reason: Lapses were still noted in the evaluation of the supporting documents attached to the payments made to the contractor in CY 2020 for the implementation of infrastructure project – Design and Build for the Upgrading/Expansion of Santa Fe Potable Water System Level III (Phase 3B).

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

LDRRM Council to ensure compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 in the preparation of the LDRRMFIP.  

Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the accounting and reporting guidelines set forth under the same Circular.  Also, make the necessary adjusting entries to correctly present the balances of the affected accounts.  

LDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant to carefully prepare the various reports (including the templates) regarding the LDRRMF to be able to provide accurate and reliable information to the readers thereof.
observations.managementAction: The 30% allocation for Quick Response Fund (QRF) was presented in lump-sum amount in the LDRRMFIP for CY 2020.   Furthermore, the projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended balances of LDRRMF of previous years were already disclosed in a separate Plan.  The Municipal Accountant also see to it that  total unutilized balances appearing in the Report on Utilization of DRRMF and the disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements for CY 2020 were reconciled.  All the equipment purchased during CY 2020 were likewise disclosed in the template – Inventory of Equipment Purchased Out of DRRMF/ Donations as of December 31, 2020.  Also, the Municipal Accountant has already set-up at year-end the amount of P1,588,885.85 representing the unexpended balances of 30% QRF and 70% Mitigation Fund – MOOE for CY 2014 for reversion to the General Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant facilitate the submission of the aforementioned lacking documents to the Audit Team.  Moreover, we recommended that they should check the completeness and correctness of the supporting documents attached to the claims for payment of infrastructure projects in compliance with the provisions cited in COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We further recommended that the Municipal Engineer make representations to the officers of TIELCO for the installation of the secondary line in order for the water system to become operational.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer see to it that the information presented in the documents/ reports issued by the Municipality are consistent.   Furthermore, substantial compliance with the documentary requirements enumerated in COA Circular No. 2012-001 was observed on the payments made to the contractor on the project – Local Access Road in Barangay Poblacion A. Medina Street during CY 2020.
observations.reason: Lapses were still noted in the evaluation of the supporting documents attached to the payments made to the contractor in CY 2020 for the implementation of infrastructure project – Design and Build for the Upgrading/ Expansion of Santa Fe Potable Water System Level III (Phase 3B).

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

direct the MPDC to appraise the socio-economic and environmental conditions of the locality and identify the programs and activities to be given priority to address the necessity of the constituents.  

direct the MPDC and the Municipal Engineer to see to it that the projects to be charged against the 20% Development Fund are in line with the allowable development projects enumerated in the DILG and DBM JMC No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 amended by JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017. 

instruct the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant to include in their reports the continuing projects of the Municipality to be able to provide complete and accurate information to the readers thereof.
observations.managementAction: All the projects identified in the AIP for CY 2020 were in accordance with Item 3.0 of the JMC.  Out of the total appropriations amounting to P15,749,065.40, projects with appropriations totaling P4,500,000.00 equivalent to 28.57% were not implemented during CY 2020.  This only showed that the Municipality see to it that majority of the PPAs charged against the 20% DF for CY 2020 were implemented during the year.  The Audit Team likewise observed that the disbursements charged against the Fund were recorded to appropriate asset accounts.
observations.reason:
The Municipality still failed to implement various projects sourced out from continuing appropriations as of December 31, 2020.   

Reiterated in Observation No. 4 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management exert effort in determining if the National Solid Waste Management Commission approved their 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan.  Moreover, comply strictly with the provisions of Republic Act No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: The Management has demonstrated various actions relative to Solid Waste Management.  During CY 2018, the Municipality implemented the project – Establishment and Operation of Material Recovery Facility funded by the DENR-EMB.  In CY 2019, the Municipality has procured 1 unit plastic shredder machine and 1 set brick moulder (steel made) totalling P296,000.00 to be used in the solid waste management.  This was sourced out from the 20% DF for CY 2018.  During CY 2020, the Municipality implemented the continuing projects namely: Construction of Septic Tanks for Wet Residuals with Geomembrane Lining, Construction of Residual Containment Area, Construction of Concrete Vault for Hazardous Waste and Rehabilitation of Solid Waste Management Facilities.  In the same year, the Municipality has purchased lots intended for the expansion of municipal disposal facility and sanitary landfill.  The Municipality has also undertaken the procurement procedures in the construction materials needed for the Rehabilitation of Controlled Dumping Site.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management promote and implement a comprehensive Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan that supports disaster risk management activities. She also recorded an entry in December 2020 for the setting-up of the amount of  P1,588,885.85 representing the unexpended balances of 30% QRF and 70% Mitigation Fund – MOOE for CY 2014 for reversion to the General Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the municipality charge to the 20% Development Fund only those expenses which were disbursed for capital expenditures and contribute to the creation of tangible physical existence of the investments allowed in the Fund. Charging against the Fund of wages of laborers and contractual personnel not contributing to the creation/addition to capital assets/investments is discouraged. Consider adding to the capital asset accounts those investment expenditures which are aligned to the Fund purposes. Rectify and adjust the affected asset accounts to reflect their correct values/costs in the succeeding financial statements of the Municipality.

On the cost of labor which are spent for the fabrication/production of capital assets for the Forest Park economic enterprises and WATSAN, we recommended that separate program of works to show the estimates for materials and labor components is needed from which to base all capital charges. Such program of works shall be attached to all the corresponding vouchers to signal that these claims need to be capitalized under the 20 DF%. 

The Municipality is also encouraged to separate from the 20% DF all non-capital outlay components and charge them from the corresponding office appropriations in the General Fund. 

We also recommended that Management return to the 20%DF the misused monies and reprogram them for other priority development projects as identified by the Local Development Council. The status of implementation of these recommendations shall be reported and monitored in succeeding audit periods.
observations.managementAction: Management has incurred the same violation in CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the LGU augment their pool of heavy equipment to address the need for readiness at all times in order to simultaneously implement projects and maximize manpower and utilization of equipment during good weather. Procurements through public bidding must be properly planned and conducted simultaneously to give opportunity to various suppliers and contractors to participate in the provision of materials and services for municipal projects. Augment also the personnel in the Engineering Office to attend to the preparation of program/project estimates and expedite the preparation of procurement documents. Additional personnel should also be hired to monitor project implementation to ensure that projects are implemented according to specifications to attain their desired and optimum quality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has not fully implemented the 20% DF projects in CY2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the privileges of men shall also be given priority and allocation of funds in succeeding annual GAD plans in compliance with the issued JMCs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management send demand letters to these persons asking for the return of the amount due from them. Strategize collection through the joint efforts of concerned municipal offices to properly return back government monies to the municipal coffers.  Otherwise, the balances shall be disallowed for lack of documents that shall warrant their liquidation considering the long period of time the funds had stayed out of government coffers and those who authorized their payments shall be solidarily liable with the payee-recipients.

In the event that deficiencies/
discrepancies exist between the Inventory Report and accounting records, the necessary adjustments/
corrections should be made.  Also, require the Inventory Committee to make a report of unserviceable/
non-functional PPEs in the Inventory and Inspection Report on Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) which could be undertaken simultaneously with the physical inventory for disposal and eventual proper relief of accountabilities.

observations.managementAction: No action was taken by Management.
observations.reason:
The Inventory Committee still failed to conduct physical count of the properties of the Municipality at year-end.   Consequently, the corresponding inventory reports could not be made.  

Reiterated in Observation No. 1 of this report.

Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly to the accounting and reporting guidelines set forth under COA Circular No. 2012-002.  

LDRRMO to submit the duly accomplished templates on the Status of DRRMF as of December 31, 2017.

coordinate with the Municipal Accountant during the preparation of reports to provide accurate information; and

Send demand letters to identified accountable officers and pursue the liquidation or settlement thereof through refund and/or withholding of salaries.

the Municipal Mayor along with all the concerned officials and employees identify the priority needs of its constituents and strictly follow the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 of the DILG and DBM as updated under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01 dated February 22, 2017 in the imple-mentation of projects/
programs/
activities for the 20% Development Fund;

the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Council to identify priority development projects that constitute the nature of capital expenditures and refrain from incorporating lump sum or generic term appropriations to avoid insertions of projects, programs and activities that are not related to the imple-mentation of the 20% Development Fund;

the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain a separate account as prescribed by Section 313 (c) of R.A. No. 7160 as well as the necessary subsidiary ledgers pertaining thereto; and

corrections should be made.  Also, require the Municipal Treasurer to make a report of unserviceable/
