observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for all the LGU’s bank accounts to the Audit Team together with the paid checks, debit/credit memos for verification as provided for under Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-011.  Also make an extra effort to submit the bank reconciliation statements on time.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by Management.
observations.reason: Significant delay was observed in the submission of monthly bank reconciliation statements.  At present, the Audit Team was not yet furnished with the bank reconciliation statements of all the bank accounts together with the bank statements, paid checks, and debit/credit memos for the months of October to December 2017.  Also, bank reconciliation statements for the Bank Account No. 1162-1024-58 under the Special Education Fund for CYs 2010 to 2014 were not yet submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor advise the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to enforce collection thereof to protect the interest of the government or request for authority from COA to write-off such long outstanding receivables through a Sanggunian Resolution, in line with COA Resolution No. 2003-002 dated January 30, 2003, stating the reasons for write-off and extent of follow-up made.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by Management.
observations.reason: Verification of the schedules of the Municipality as of December 31, 2017 disclosed that the dormant accounts with a long outstanding balance of P1,030,076.28 still remained in the books under the Other Receivables account.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers in accordance with paragraph e, Section 4 of NGAS Manual, Volume I and Section 10 of the same Manual, Volume II.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by Management.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant submitted monthly schedules of real accounts, however, those were not supported with subsidiary ledgers.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant was advised to give priority to the timely submission of the accounts and financial reports within the reglementary period as required under COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009, COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 and Section 70, Volume I of the MNGAS for LGUs to enable the Audit Team to conduct timely audit thereof and promptly inform the Management of the deficiencies noted, if any.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by Management.
observations.reason: Analysis of the submission of the accounts and monthly trial balances showed that the Accounting Office submitted within the reglementary period only for the month of November.  The submission of the remaining months incurred significant delay.  The most significant delay was observed in the submission of the accounts and trial balances for the month of December.  Records of the Office showed that those were submitted only on March 28, 2018 along with the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to exert extra efforts to identify the breakdown of P254,194.30 balance from 2007.  Send demand letters to identified accountable officers and pursue the liquidation or settlement thereof through refund and/or withholding of salaries.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by Management.
observations.reason: The balance amounting P254,194.30 pertaining to prior years’ cash advances remained unliquidated as of December 31, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, as the internal auditor, should see to it that all the necessary documentary requirements attached to the vouchers are accurate and complete before processing the claims.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by Management.
observations.reason: Same observations were noted in CY 2017 audit.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

The Municipal Mayor ensure adequate allocation of at least five percent (5%) of the total budget appropriations for priority programs, projects and activities that address gender issues or women’s concerns; and

The GAD Focal Point System members should undergo gender sensitivity training and GAD Planning and Budgeting to address the various findings of DILG MIMAROPA in their GAD Plan and Budget.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by Management.
observations.reason: Verification of the budgets revealed that the GAD appropriations is less than the amount that should have been appropriated for GAD.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor along with all the concerned officials and employees strictly follow, among others, Section 5.0 of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 in the implementation of programs/projects for the 20% Development Fund.  See to it that those development projects identified in the Annual Investment Plan were fully implemented for the benefit of the constituents.  We further recommended that Municipal Mayor implement programs/ projects/
activities that are in line with social and economic development as well as environmental management concerns of the community in accordance with the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 of the DILG and DBM.  Stop the charging of expenses which do not constitute capital expenditures from the 20% Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by Management.
observations.reason:
Same observations were noted in CY 2017 audit. 

Reiterated in Finding No. 7 of this report.

observations.recommendation: 1.7	We recommended that the constituted Inventory Committee (1) conduct immediately physical inventory taking of all municipal properties, (2) prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment as at December 31, 2017, (3) submit the RPCPPE to the COA as required in consonance with the above-mentioned regulations.

2.9	We recommended that the OIC-Accountant reconcile the general ledger figures with the distributed amounts in the subsidiary ledgers by (1) tracing entries starting at the time they could not any more prepare subsidiary details due to accounts fragmentation, (2) distributing the lump sum amounts lodged in a separate subsidiary ledger, and, (3) adjusting the affected accounts for errors made, in order to come up with faithful reporting of liability accounts that could improve the fairness with which the financial statements are prepared.

3.12	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (1) consistently prepare the monthly bank reconciliation statements, (2) immediately adjust the books for valid reconciling items, in order to attain the accurate and reliable reporting of the total balance of depository accounts as at the end of the month.

4.11	We recommended that Management (1) trace the movement of the RPT and SET Receivables and corresponding Deferred Credits, (2) segregate from the Report of Monthly Collections the 25% for the Barangay, 35% for the Province and 40% for the Municipality on RPT-Basic, (3) segregate from the same Report of Monthly Collections the 50% for the Province and the 50% for the Municipality on SET, (4) prepare JEV to adjust the affected accounts based on the results of segregating the amounts of shares of the Barangays, Province and Municipality, (5) prepare the appropriate JEV entry following the pro-forma entry for tax collections in 2018 and in the succeeding periods in order to come up with the true balance of collectibles from taxpayers as at December 31, 2018.

5.5	We recommended that (1) the Municipal officials plan the proper recovery of audit disallowances by applying the provisions of COA Resolution 2017-021 dated November 3, 2017  in the instalment settlement of accounts; (2) prepare cash flow projections from the collection of disallowances per quarter as a target source of additional funds, (3) coordinate with the Disbursing Officer to withhold from the net take home pays of persons liable the agreed semi-monthly/monthly settlements, (4) conduct continuous reminders during municipal assemblies such as Monday flag raising ceremonies on the payments of disallowances, and, (5) see to it that all audit disallowances shall have been collected as at the end of the fiscal year 2018, to protect the best interest of the government and safeguard its scarce resources.

6.6	We recommended that (1) the Municipal Treasurer record in the Report of Checks Issued only those checks which were already released and acknowledged by payees during the period; (2) the Municipal Accountant record in the Check Disbursements Journal only those checks verified to be issued to the payees as evidenced by their duly dated acknowledgement receipt on the last box of the disbursement voucher; stop the practice of portraying that liabilities were already settled as at the end of the period when actual payment was not yet delivered, in order to reflect the true balance of the depository account with the AGDB.

7.6     We recommended that the Accounting Office (1) trace back whether the transactions of prior years were recorded in reciprocity by preparing subsidiary ledgers per fund per account, (2) prepare working paper showing the discrepancies in recording the affected accounts, (3) adjust the accounts based on the cumulative effects to accounts as concluded in the working papers, and (4) submit copy of the WP to COA as part of the agency compliance to AAR recommendations  to be reported in the AAPSI.

8.7	We recommended that at the end of succeeding accounting periods, the Municipality should determine in full all unpaid obligations to be accrued  in the books in order to correctly state the amount of Expenses, Liabilities and Government Equity as at the end of the year and prevent the misstatement of the financial position of the Municipality.

9.7	We recommended that the Local Finance Committee whose major role and function is to manage the sources and uses of municipal funds should abide by the provisions of the Local Government Code, in planning the appropriation and disbursement of municipal moneys, in order that the municipality can implement its target, plans and projects through the maximum utilization and application of municipal funds to provide quality services to its officials, employees and the public as a whole. Return to the municipal coffers the P1,881,712.50 cost of excess Personnel Benefits to be issued with a Notice of Disallowance  to enforce settlement thereof.

10.8	We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Liquidating Officer deposit intact their collections daily especially on the last transaction day of every fiscal year in order to safekeep municipal funds at the depository bank and prevent the possibility of loss of scarce resources  through theft and the temptation to misuse funds belonging to the government during the festive season.

11.9	We recommended that the Municipality limit their level of expenditures for Personnel Services in FY 2017 in conformity with the provisions of Section 325 (a) of RA No. 7160 and Section 7 of Joint Resolution of Congress in order to distribute limited municipal resources to various vital purposes with the end in view of attaining optimum delivery of basic services to the constituents.

12.6	We recommended that (1) Management should be discreet in disbursing government funds such that trust receipts should only be used for their intended purposes, (2) refrain from granting cash advances in the Trust Fund unless the nature of the trust receipt requires that it is implemented through the assistance of a disbursing officer, (3) encourage the liquidation/refund of these cash advances to return back to the fund the misused trust receipts.

13.12	We recommended that Management (1) comply strictly with the above-cited COA rules and regulations, (2) prepare a semi-monthly schedule of cash advance payments to be withheld per accountable officer which shall be strictly implemented until full payment of accountabilities at the end of 2018. Likewise, (3) enforce full settlement of cash advances to be granted in FY2018 to reflect that the municipality is indeed ready to receive subsidies for development projects.

15.9	We recommended that the Municipality implement the allotments of the 20% Development Fund in accordance with the prescribed purposes of the JMC which shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures only to enhance the full development of the people of the Municipality of Santa Fe.

16.4	We recommended that the Municipal Development Council identify the projects which are not useful or might give less benefit to the people out of the 20% Development Fund programs, projects and activities  and reprogram the funds in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC 2017-1 and implement in full the continuing PPAs in order to support the socio-economic development of the constituency.

17.5	We recommended that the Management attach all relevant supporting documents to transactions to enable the COA to render the proper result of evaluation which is needed by Management in making decisions. The Municipality is then assured that it is paying the commensurate amount of project cost on the actual stage of project completion. Likewise, COA issues either Notice of Suspension or Notice of Disallowance as a result of non-submission of documentary requirements as prescribed in COA Circular 2012-001 and Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.

18.4	We recommended that the Municipal Engineer prepare the annual programs of works of 20% DF programs, projects and activities especially the stages where procurement of materials is required in order to discourage piecemeal shopping purchases of project requirements, instead, procure through competitive bidding to avail of additional income in the form of savings on bulk purchases and the Municipality is also assured of buying items with appropriate quality and specifications.

19.4	We recommended that the BAC shall be discreet and cautious in the accomplishment of procurement papers especially the documents that indicate the obligations, duties and responsibilities of contractors to protect the best interest of the municipal government, its purposes and its funds.

20.7	We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer (1) report and account all issued accountable forms, (2) refrain from maintaining cash reserves in the municipal coffers,  (3) deposit in full and intact all collections from the issuance of accountable forms, (4) the collectors remit daily and in full all collections of the Municipality, and (5) the Municipal Treasurer establish through the issuance of memorandum to all collectors the policies and guidelines in reporting and managing their collections such that a cut-off time should be observed daily to delineate the period when collections are reported and accounted in the municipal coffers which should be convenient for the Liquidating Officer to deposit them daily and intact with the authorized government depository bank in accordance with the pertinent provisions of the Revised Cash Examination Manual in order to safeguard government funds from the risk of possible loss.

21.9	We recommended that the Liquidating Officer (1) report and account all issued accountable forms, (2) refrain from maintaining cash reserves in the municipal coffers,  (3) deposit in full and intact all collections from the issuance of accountable forms in accordance with the pertinent provisions of the Revised Cash Examination Manual in order to safeguard government funds from the risk of possible loss.

22.4	We recommended that (1) Management should be discreet in granting personnel benefits only to qualified and eligible employees as provided by laws, rules and regulations despite good intentions of giving humane benefits due to economic hardships experienced in the nation, and (2)  Refrain from granting benefits to job orders and contract of services because of the absence of a valid employer-employee relationship.

23.8	We recommended that the Municipality implement the planned programs and activities for the prevention and mitigation of disasters,  in order that it ensures its readiness to manage the risks brought about by future disasters and is able to protect well the welfare of its constituents.

24.20	We recommended that in the review of every government transaction prior to payment, the Municipal Accountant should attach a checklist of required supporting documents peculiar to the nature of the transaction upon which pre-audit on the completeness of attachments is checked, otherwise, the voucher is returned for want of documents and shall be certified for payment upon full submission of the requirements thereof. The Municipal Treasurer should see to it that all documentary requirements peculiar to each transaction are attached before issuing payment.

25.9	We recommended that (1) the Municipal Treasurer stop issuing checks to the Disbursing Officer to fund the payment of RATA in the first week of the month, (2) the Local Chief Executive and the Vice-Mayor stop authorizing early payments of RATA, (3) release RATA benefits only upon rendition of full services by the municipal officials or claims may be prorated based on actual work/attendance and performance, (4) the officials concerned refund in full any excess claim for RATA as a result of incurring deductible absences within the month, in consonance with the above rules and regulations in order to prevent the advance use of municipal money that earns income while held in deposit at the Bank to the best advantage of the municipality.

26.8	We recommended that (1) Municipal officials refrain from reserving the RATA to prioritize personal purposes but their gain shall be the unused remainder after spending for official functions of a month, (2) refrain from deducting from RATA payrolls the repayments for personal loans or any kind of contribution, the RATA shall be free from any encumbrances, (3) officials assigned with government vehicle should stop claiming the Transportation Allowance, and (4) officials using government vehicles should allow prorated deduction from their Transportation Allowance based on the number of days of use in consonance with LBC No. 103 to prevent double charging of the cost of fuel which could be from the TA and at the same time from the payment of fuel to the supplier.
27.1  We recommended that the municipality abide with governmental regulations on fund transfer to Non-Government Organizations/People’s Organizations.
28.1   We recommended that GAD programs, projects and activities must always be aligned with the above provisions and they must address gender-related issues.
29.1  We recommended that the municipality comply with the rules and regulations on the availment of loan and its proper recording in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
require the Inventory Committee to prepare and submit to the Audit Team the RPCI and the RPCPPE if indeed, the physical count was conducted and
thereafter, instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the resulting balance of the physical count with the accounting records in order to present the correct balances of the said accounts in the financial statements.
In the event that deficiencies/discrepancies exist between the Inventory Report and the accounting records, the necessary adjustments/corrections should be made.  Also, require the Inventory Committee to make a report of unserviceable/non-functional PPEs in the Inventory and Inspection Report on Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) which could be undertaken simultaneously with the physical inventory for disposal and request for relief from accountabilities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor strictly observe the general limitations on personal services cited under Section 325 (a) of R.A. No. 7160 and Local Budget Circular No. 98 dated October 14, 2011.  Also, she should always be guided by the conditions of the agency’s annual and supplemental budgets to ensure that the budgeting requirements are completely complied with.  As part of her fiscal responsibility, she should allow only payments of personal services costs and other expenditures expressly authorized by law.

We recommended that the Municipal Vice-Mayor limit the participants in the events conducted by their respective leagues.
Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer see to it that the claims of the officials are within the available funds of their department.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to remit to GSIS the withheld contributions and loan amortizations intact and within the prescribed period in compliance with Sections 6 and 7 of R.A. No. 8291.  Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant record properly the withholding and remittance of contributions and premiums.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to remit to Pag-IBIG the withheld contributions and loan amortizations intact and within the prescribed period.  Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant record properly the withholding and remittance of contributions.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to remit to PhilHealth the withheld contributions intact and within the prescribed period to avoid incurrence of penalties.  Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant record properly the withholding and remittance of contributions.

We recommended that the BAC incorporate all matters that have been discussed in the Minutes.
We recommended that the BAC thoroughly check the contents of the Bidding Documents to provide consistency of information.
We recommended that the BAC refrain from making communications with the prospective bidder with the intention of avoiding failure of bidding.
We recommended that the BAC show consistency of information on the documents submitted to the Audit Team.
We recommended that the BAC fully accomplish the checklists being used during the bid opening.
We recommended that the BAC update its checklists in conformity with the documentary requirements prescribed by the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.
We recommended that the BAC examine the completeness of the supporting documents of the SLCC during the bid opening.
We recommended that the BAC conduct a detailed bid evaluation.
We recommended that the BAC include, in its discussion during the pre-bid conference, the prescribed format of the statement of all its ongoing government and private contracts.
We recommended that the BAC validate the documents submitted by the bidder whether it conform or not with the bidding documents issued.
We recommended that the BAC check the consistency of information provided in the documents submitted by the bidder during the post-qualification process.
We recommended that the BAC thoroughly check the performance security posted by the contractor before contract signing.
We recommended strict compliance of the BAC with Section 37.2.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.
We recommended that the BAC should be aware of the 5-day minimum requirement prescribed by Section 13.3 of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 regarding the date of receipt of the invitation letter.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC to:
strictly comply with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.
judiciously check the documents submitted by the bidders.
be cautious in preparing documents to show consistency of information to the readers of those documents.

In view of the foregoing audit observations, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) to comply strictly with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly to the accounting and reporting guidelines set forth under COA Circular No. 2012-002.
LDRRMO to submit the duly accomplished templates on the Status of DRRMF as of December 31, 2017.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC to:
strictly comply with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.
judiciously check the documents submitted by the bidders.
be cautious in preparing documents to show consistency of information to the readers of those documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
direct the MPDC to appraise the socio-economic and environmental conditions of the locality and identify the programs and activities to be given priority to address the necessity of the constituents.
direct the MPDC and the Municipal Engineer to see to it that the projects to be charged against the 20% Development Fund are in line with the allowable development projects enumerated in the DILG and DBM JMC No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 amended by JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.
instruct the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant to include in their reports the continuing projects of the Municipality to be able to provide complete and accurate information to the readers thereof.
The Audit Team also verified the compliance of the BAC with the provisions of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and observed that:
The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) committed procedural lapses in the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects charged against the 20% Development Fund which were not in accordance with the provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of Republic Act (R.A.) No. 9184, otherwise known as the Government Procurement Reform Act, thus, affecting the propriety of the contracts.
In terms of contract cost, the most significant projects subjected to review by the Audit Team are as follows:
Review of the above projects disclosed various deficiencies.  Below is the common observation noted in the contracts:
The BAC failed to post the Notice of Award (NoA) in the PhilGEPS within three calendar days from its issuance as prescribed by Section 37.1.6 of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.  This is common to Contract Nos. 1 and 2.
In the reply of the BAC Chairman, he reasoned out that this is due to network problem and the inaccessibility of the PhilGEPS website especially during office hours.  This is also affirmed by the Head of the BAC-Technical Working Group (TWG) during the exit conference.  He added that the BAC Secretariat sometimes stayed until dawn to be able to post in the PhilGEPS.  He also said that the website is always under system maintenance.
Aside from the common observation, the Audit Team also noted other deficiencies in the three contracts which were discussed separately.
... (See detailed observations on specific contracts and POs in the report.)
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC to:
strictly comply with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.
judiciously check the documents submitted by the bidders.
be cautious in preparing documents to show consistency of information to the readers of those documents.

In light of the foregoing observations, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GFPS to:
carefully prepare the GAD Plan and Budget of the Municipality to fully address the gender issues and concerns within and outside the organization that would have great impact to the intended beneficiaries in the long run;
coordinate with the Municipal Accountant during the preparation of reports to provide accurate information; and
comply strictly with the guidelines in the preparation and submission of LGU GAD AR provided in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 amended by JMC No. 2016-01.
The Audit Team also verified the compliance of the BAC with the provisions of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and observed that:
The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) committed procedural lapses in the procurement activities undertaken for the purchases of materials on the projects charged against the GAD Fund which were not in accordance with the provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of Republic Act (R.A.) No. 9184, otherwise known as the Government Procurement Reform Act, thus, affecting the propriety of the contracts.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC to:
strictly comply with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.
judiciously check the documents submitted by the bidders.
be cautious in preparing documents to show consistency of information to the readers of those documents.

We recommend that the BAC submit the complete bidding documents
and the eligibility requirements of the above-cited transaction for evaluation
by this office to avoid eventual suspension of the transactions.

We also recommend that henceforth, the BAC and Management award
procurement contracts only to qualified suppliers and ensure that all pertinent
documentary requirements relative to the municipality’s procurements are
complete and adequately made available.

We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee through the
BAC Secretariat provide justification for the absence of invitation to
independent observers as required under RA 9184. Moreover, ensure
that notices or invitations are served to the minimum required
independent observers with appropriate proof of receipt pursuant to
Section 13 of RA 9184.

We recommend that Management submit the Memorandum of
Agreement (MOA) duly executed by and between the Municipality of
Santa Fe, Cebu and the DILG and BFAR and Special Allotment Release
Order (SARO) to determine the standards of implementation of the
different projects and whether the BuB projects were implemented in
accordance with such standards.

We recommend that the Chief Executive direct the Inventory
Team/Committee to complete the physical inventory taking of all
existing properties of the Municipality to obtain the correct valuation of
the property, plant and equipment and submit to the Auditor an updated
inventory report on the physical count of the properties not later than
January 31 of each year pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS Manual,
Volume I. Continued failure on the part of the officials concerned to
submit the reports mentioned shall automatically cause the suspension
of payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the
requirements of the Commission by virtue of Par. 2, Section 122 of P.D.
1445.

We recommend that Management submit justification for the non
disqualification of the winning bidders who failed to post the
Performance Security within the reglementary period, otherwise, the
person responsible for the transactions should be held accountable
pursuant to the penalties and sanctions provided in RA 9184 and its
revised IRR.

We recommend that Management submit the justification for the
deficiency of the transaction, otherwise, persons responsible for the
inordinate proceeding should be held accountable pursuant to the
penalties and administrative sanctions provided for under RA 9184 and
its Implementing Rules and Regulations.

Furthermore, we recommend that Management exert due
diligence in the conduct of its procurement proceedings and ensure that
provisions of the procurement are strictly adhered to avoid personal
liability.

We recommend that Management charge expenditures for
purchase of administrative equipment and facilities under the
Municipality’s General Fund and to refrain from using the 20%
Development Fund for ineligible items that do not fall under the
guidelines set forth under the DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2017-1, otherwise, all disbursements in violation of the joint
memorandum circular will be a ground for disallowance in audit. The
fund must be utilized only for priority projects whose benefits redound
to the majority of its constituents.

Furthermore, we recommend that Management demand and
collect the liquidated damages from the supplier, otherwise, a notice of
disallowance will be issued to this effect.

We recommend that the Municipality prepare an allocation and
distribution of the proceeds of Tourism Ecological Fee pursuant to the
Municipal Ordinance No. 05-2017 to effect the proper recognition of
income and optimize the usage of the funds.

We recommend that Management speed up the implementation of
said projects and programs funded under the Bottom-Up Budgeting
(BuB) and ensure that funds are optimally utilized with a view of
ensuring efficiency, economy and effectiveness in the utilization of
government resources for the benefit of its constituencies. Otherwise,
Management should return such fund to the source agency so that
appropriate agencies could utilize the same for other equally important
government projects.

We recommend that Management refrain from indicating in the
Purchase Request and Request for Quotation the specific brand name
when purchasing equipment and other items. Likewise, strict
compliance of Section 18 of RA 9184 should be observed.

We recommend that the concerned municipal official be directed
to refund the amount of additional compensation or allowance received.
A Notice of Disallowance will be issued to the effect.

We also recommend that, henceforth, strict compliance with the
provisions of Section 95 of RA 7160 and COA Circular No. 2013-003 and
other pertinent laws is enjoined to avoid personal liability.

We recommend that Management through the Human Resource
Management Office (HRMO) evaluate the Municipality’s manpower
structure and present system on the hiring and determining of wage
rate of its Job Order workers and consequently establish appropriate
guidelines or policies to ensure that hiring and wage rate of JO workers
are in accordance with the agency’s needs, service requirements,
financial capabilities and of the workers capabilities and qualifications
pursuant to the minimum standards set forth by government regulating
bodies with a view of ensuring efficiency, economy and effectiveness in
its operation and the disposition of government’s scarce resources.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor consider that the hiring
of job order personnel be based on necessity and should be
identified/attributed to specific projects for a period not exceeding six
months pursuant to Section 2, Rule XI of the Revised Omnibus Rules on
Appointments and Other Personnel Actions, under CSC Memorandum
Circular No. 40 and Section 77 of R.A. 7160.

Furthermore, we recommend that Management through the
Human Resource Management Office (HRMO) evaluate the
Municipality’s manpower structure and present system on the hiring of
its Job Order workers and consequently establish appropriate
guidelines or policies to ensure that hiring are in accordance with the
agency’s needs, service requirements, financial capabilities and of the
workers capabilities and qualifications pursuant to provisions set forth
by government regulating bodies with a view of ensuring efficiency,
economy and effectiveness in its operation and the disposition of
government’s scarce resources. Likewise, maintain adequate personnel
records to facilitate easy verification and monitoring of the agency’s
manpower resources.

We recommend that Management execute a yearly contract of
lease embodied in a public instrument for every public market stall
occupant outlining the terms and conditions as well as effectivity
thereof to ensure that the interests of both parties are protected
pursuant to COA Circular No. 88-282A.

We recommend that Management create a Solid Waste
Management Board in accordance with Section 12 of RA 9003 to ensure
proper Solid Waste Management Operation. We also recommend that
Management provide necessary support in enjoining the barangays to
establish their own MRF.

We further recommend that Management secure the approval of
the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan from the National Solid Waste
Management Commission and to strictly implement the Municipal’s
Solid Waste Ordinance.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal
Accountant prepare reports on the operation and Financial Statements
for the Fish Drying Facilities Project under the Economic Enterprises
Operation. We also recommend that Management maintain subsidiary
ledger and submit the trial balance and Financial Statements pursuant
to the Local Government Code and The Manual on New Government
Accounting System (NGAS) for Local Government Units. Failure to
submit the pertinent FS could be a ground for the withholding of salary.

We further recommend that Management come up with an
operational guideline which will be used in the day to day operation of
the Fish Drying Facilities Project.

We recommend that appropriation for the LCPC be utilized in
accordance with the provisions of DILG provided Memorandum Circular
No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012 and avoid utilizing the said funds for
administrative expenditures chargeable to the general fund’s MOOE that
could otherwise deplete the said funds without attaining its objectives.

We recommended that management:

stop the practice of undertaking trainings, seminars, workshops or similar activities in places outside of their official station or territorial jurisdiction funded out of public funds.  
strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 85-55A dated September 8, 1985 and COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2011-59 dated April 25, 2011. 
stop incurrence of the same expenditure on 2017 onward, for this will be disallowed.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management comply with the proper utilization of LDRRMF and ensure that the programs and projects to be funded for the 70% disaster preparedness and mitigation fund be those that will support disaster risk management activities as provided under Section 3.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management utilize the 30% QRF only if there is a resolution of the Local Sanggunian declaring that the LGU is under the state of calamity. Likewise, 30% QRF should not be used for pre – disaster programs, projects and activities.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation. No declaration of calamity during the year, hence, QRF was transferred to the Trust Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management fast track the processing of their requirements for the DENR’s Initial Environmental Examination so that permit for the operation of sanitary landfill may be released the soonest possible time to avert adverse environmental consequences.
observations.managementAction: Dumpsite was improved but sanitary landfill was not yet constructed though already begun.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accounting Office submit the accounts of the municipality within the prescribed period for timely review and evaluation of transactions as to their propriety, completeness and accuracy. Further, said Office should strictly observe the aforementioned regulation pertaining to submission of accounts to avoid possible administrative disciplinary action and disallowance of transactions involved.
observations.managementAction: Submission of municipal accounts is on time, hence, timely post-audit could be made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and certify a monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF in order for the LDRRMO to submit said report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the resident auditor.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMF Report was not submitted on time to the COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accountable Officer prepare separate cash books and separate reports on collections and deposits for the two funds under her accountability in compliance with the above – cited provisions of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer maintains the SEF cashbook for collections.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that officials/employees on official travel obtain a certification from the head of the agency to justify the necessity of the hotel/lodging expense if such is in excess of what is set by law in conformity with Section 4 of Executive Order No. 298 dated March 23, 2004 and Section 1.2.4 of COA Circular 2012 – 001.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 on the specifications and relevant information on the posting of signboard of program, project or activity regardless of amount or source of funds in order to fully adopt to the policy of full disclosure of all transactions involving public interest.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management enforce the return of unused petty cash to the treasurer as required under Section 48 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Vol. I.
observations.managementAction: No Petty Cash balance could be found in the FS as at year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enforce settlement of these disallowances in compliance to Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006.
observations.managementAction: Management was not able to collect in full the subject Receivables.  Only a minimal amount was received in settlement of SEF NDs.
Management was able to reduce the level of unliquidated cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the officials responsible in processing payrolls that its vouchers be supported with complete documentary requirements in adherence to the above cited provisions to ensure the validity of the claims.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the required documents. However, accomplishment reports of JOs and casuals were sometimes presented in inclusive dates and not on a per day basis which is required since wages are paid per day of accomplishment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management to enforce the return of unused petty cash fund at year-end to the treasurer as required under Section 48 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System, Vol. I and a new cash advance for petty cash shall be set up in the ensuing year.
observations.managementAction: The management returned the unused petty cash fund but the remaining P2,010.00 shall be returned this February.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor:

require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of all Inventories and Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) accounts of the Municipality and submit the corresponding report to the Audit Team; and

thereafter, instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the resulting balance of the physical count with the accounting records in order to present the correct balances of said accounts in the financial statements.

In the event that deficiencies/
discrepancies exist between the Inventory Report and accounting records, the necessary adjustments/
corrections should be made.  Also, require the Head of the Inventory Committee to make a report of unserviceable/
non-functional PPEs in the IIRUP which could be undertaken simultaneous with the physical inventory for disposal and eventual proper relief of accountabilities.
observations.managementAction: The Management conducted physical count of properties at year-end.
observations.reason:
At present, the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) and the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) were not yet submitted to the Audit Team.  Thus, reconciliation between the results of the physical count and the trial balances could not be made. 

Reiterated in Finding No. 1 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management stop the practice of recognizing fund transfers received for specific purpose as regular income to avoid incurring excess of PS cost over PS limitation.

We further recommended that Management strictly observe the general limitations on personal services cited under Section 325 (a) of R.A. No. 7160 and Local Budget Circular No. 98 dated October 14, 2011.  The Municipal Mayor, being the Local Chief Executive, should always be guided by the conditions of the agency’s annual and supplemental budgets to ensure that the budgeting requirements are completely complied with.  As part of her fiscal responsibility, she should allow payments of personal services costs and other expenditures only expressly authorized by law.
observations.managementAction: The Management already stopped the practice of recognizing fund transfers received for specific purpose as regular income.
observations.reason:
For CY 2017, the Municipality exceeded its (55%) Personal Services Limitation/Cap by P4,336,177.83 per budget and P1,145,749.03 per actual expenditure.

Reiterated in Finding No. 2 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management exert effort in determining if the National Solid Waste Management Commission approved their 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan.  Moreover, comply strictly with the provisions of Republic Act No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality already sent a letter to the National Solid Waste Management Commission requesting status of the second plan that was submitted to them.  Furthermore, a garbage truck was purchased during the year to be utilized for the collection of solid wastes.  The Municipality also purchased a lot amounting to P500,000.00 which is approximately 1.7 hectares adjacent to the existing dumpsite.  This land is purposely for the landfill, however, it is still for inspection by the Environmental Management Bureau.
observations.reason: The Municipality is still operating on a controlled dumpsite as their final disposal site.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to utilize the SEF in pursuance to the purposes for which it was established.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality adhered with Section 272 of R.A. No. 7160 in the disbursement of the funds.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management refrain from paying terminal leave benefits of officials and employees who are not yet resigned, retired or separated from service.

Also, the Municipal Accountant should ensure the completeness of documents for all payments to be made by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: During the year, no terminal leave benefits were paid to elective officials who are not yet separated from service.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management stop the practice of granting honoraria to the abovementioned officials.
observations.managementAction: The payment of honorarium to the Revenue Collection Officer and Coast Guard Officer was already stopped.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management promote and implement a comprehensive Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan that supports disaster risk management activities.  Likewise, the Municipal Accountant should strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 for the proper recording of transactions pertaining to the LDRRM Fund.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had already transferred during the year the unexpended balance of 30% QRF and DRRM-MOOE for CY 2016.
observations.reason:
The projects and activities to be charged against the previous years’ unexpended balance of the LDRRMF were still not presented under a separate caption in the LDRRMFIP for CY 2017.

Reiterated in Finding No. 6 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC to comply strictly with the requirements of the 2009 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The BAC implemented some of the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Lapses were still noted in the review of the procurement activities of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC to comply strictly with the requirements of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The BAC implemented some of the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Lapses were still noted in the review of the procurement activities of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

the Local Chief Executive ensure adequate allocation of at least five percent (5%) of the total budget appropriations for priority programs, projects and activities that address gender issues or women’s concerns; and

the GAD Focal Point System members ensure that PPAs incorporated in the GAD Plan and Budget constitute gender issues and ensure proper implementation and monitoring thereof.
observations.managementAction:
The GAD appropriations appearing in the GAD Plan and Budget is at least 5% of the total budget appropriations as prescribed by the DBM/NEDA/
NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1.  Also, the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) already conducted Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) assessment on the attributed programs during the budgeting and implementation phases.  The GAD Accomplishment Report (AR) was also supported with the necessary documents except for the actions taken by the Municipality on the COA audit findings and recommendations.
observations.reason:
Verification of the budgets revealed that the GAD appropriations is less than the amount that should have been appropriated for GAD.  The Audit Team also observed discrepancies on the amounts appearing between the GAD AR and the List of Vouchers Charged Against the GAD Fund.  The former is certified correct by the Municipal Accountant while the latter is prepared by the Technical Working Group-Chairman. 

Reiterated in Finding No. 8 of this report.

observations.recommendation:

require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of all Inventories and Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) accounts of the Municipality so that the corresponding report can be submitted to the Audit Team;  and

thereafter, instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the resulting balance of the physical count with the accounting records in order to present the correct balances of said accounts in the financial statements.

In the event that deficiencies/
discrepancies exist between the Inventory Report and accounting records, the necessary adjustments/
corrections should be made.  Also, require the Municipal Treasurer to make a report of unserviceable/
non-functional PPEs in the IIRUP which could be undertaken simultaneous with the physical inventory for disposal and eventual proper relief of accountabilities.
observations.managementAction: The Management conducted physical count of properties at year-end.
observations.reason:
At present, the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) and the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) were not yet submitted to the Audit Team.  Thus, reconciliation between the results of the physical count and the trial balances could not be made. 

Reiterated in Finding No. 1 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
The Municipal Treasurer:

Instruct all collectors to prepare RCDs and remit all collections to the Municipal Treasurer in accordance with Section 29, Vol. 1 of the NGAS Manual;

Be more circumspect in the acknowledgment of remittances in terms of accuracy of the amount;

Refrain from allowing incomplete remittances from the collecting officers.

The Municipal Accountant:

Ensure the accuracy of the amounts of recorded transactions and adjust the balance of the accounts affected by the miscalculated collections; and 

The practice of charging the unremitted collections to receivable account be refrained as this only encourages delayed remittance and/or incomplete remittance or non-remittance at all of the collections receipted by the collecting officers.
observations.managementAction: No unremitted collections was observed during the year.
observations.reason: The Report of Collections and Deposits were not prepared daily.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer submit the required approval from the Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF) authorizing the opening of the depository account other than the authorized government depository bank and consequently adhere to the limitations set forth by the circular to avoid any risks in government funds, otherwise the Municipality must transfer all funds and cash balances to banks that meet the requirements provided in said circular.
observations.managementAction: The fund is intended for Emergency Shelter Assistance (ESA) beneficiaries, who were victims of Typhoon Yolanda. The Municipal Government of Santa Fe wanted to provide the beneficiaries the easiest way of encashing their checks thus, the amount was deposited to the bank nearest to Santa Fe, which happens to be ASPAC (Asia Pacific).

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management ensure that the 20% development fund is optimally utilized to benefit the Municipality’s constituencies and that each project or program should indicate the target implementation and completion schedules for evaluation and monitoring purposes.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the contractor to complete expeditiously the said project and impose sanctions and penalties in accordance with the approved contract. Otherwise, Management thru the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) should terminate the contract in accordance with the provisions provided for in RA 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations. Furthermore, we recommend that all procurements should invariably bear complete dates of approval as well as adhere to the documentary requirements pursuant to provisions stipulated in the Philippine Bidding Documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management reevaluate the LDRRMP and ensure that the utilization of the LDRRMF is maximized through the implementation of its programs, projects or activities. Likewise, adherence to the guidelines on the utilization of LDRRMF is enjoined.
observations.managementAction: Projects, programs and activities (PPAs) for 2016 was prioritized thru the conduct of a participatory MDRRM Planning Workshop participated by the different functionaries and stakeholders in the Municipality and the PPAs was thoroughly reviewed according to its relevance to current DRRM/CCM concerns of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management establish the ownership of the machine by securing the Memorandum of Agreement executed by both parties and other pertinent documents in coordination with the Department of Science and Technology and for the municipal accountant to make appropriate adjustments if so warranted. Adherence to the generally accepted accounting principles and standards in the recording of financial transactions is enjoined.
observations.managementAction: DOST has already issued a certification dated February 13, 2017 on the transfer of ownership of Fish Dryer Machine to the Municipality of Santa Fe.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management thru the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) justify the use of alternative methods of procurement including the conditions to warrant the same and submit to this office for evaluation. Furthermore, we recommend that Management prohibit from splitting purchases to circumvent procedural purchase limits and to strictly adhere to the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 without prejudice to the offenses and penalties arising there from.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the accountable officer submit all supporting documents on the disbursements made, the official receipts pertaining to the above cited sales and the corresponding relief from accountability of the reported damaged and lost items. observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant exercise due diligence in both check issuance and recording of same and install appropriate control measures to safeguard government funds pursuant to PD 1445. observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management adhere to the documentary requirements pursuant to COA Circular 2012-001 for procurement involving public bidding. Failure of the Management to adhere shall be a ground for issuance of notice of suspension.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal General Services Officer/Municipal Treasurer conduct a complete physical count of properties, prepare and submit to the Auditor an updated inventory report on the physical count of the properties not later than January 31 of each year pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I. observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management secure the approval of the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan from the National Solid Waste Management Commission and to strictly implement the Municipal’s Solid Waste Ordinance. observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Accounting Office maintain sub-accounts consisting of separate sets of ledgers for each of the enterprise and consequently prepare separate trial balances and financial statements reflecting the financial condition and results of their respective operations, in accordance with existing regulations. We further recommend that Management should re-evaluate the viability of the Economic Enterprise to ascertain, in concrete data, whether or not it has effectively attained/served its primary purpose and to evaluate the cost effectiveness of its operating systems and procedures in the management of its equipment.
observations.managementAction: The LGU shall study whether to engage in waterworks system in particular, as local transactions, the economic enterprise shall be self-liquidating and must not represent a drain on the LGU’s resources. The setting up and operation of the said economic enterprise shall be included in development planning and investment programming. Thus, the annual slice of the funding requirements for a proposed economic enterprise shall also be considered first in the annual budget of the LGU. (Example: Feasibility Study, Survey Expenses, etc.)

observations.recommendation:

observations.reason: The Municipality still failed to fully utilize the appropriations on 20% Development Fund for CY 2016.  Moreover, there are still disbursements charged against the 20% Development Fund which do not partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures.

adherence to the guidelines on the utilization of LDRRMF is enjoined.

We recommend that Management establish the ownership of the
machine by securing the Memorandum of Agreement executed by both
parties and other pertinent documents in coordination with the
Department of Science and Technology and for the municipal
accountant to make appropriate adjustments if so warranted. Adherence
to the generally accepted accounting principles and standards in the
recording of financial transactions is enjoined.

We recommend that Management thru the Bids and Awards
Committee (BAC) justify the use of alternative methods of procurement
including the conditions to warrant the same and submit to this office
for evaluation. Furthermore, we recommend that Management prohibit
from splitting purchases to circumvent procedural purchase limits and
to strictly adhere to the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184
without prejudice to the offenses and penalties arising there from.

We recommend that Management secure the approval of the 10-
year Solid Waste Management Plan from the National Solid Waste
Management Commission and to strictly implement the Municipal’s
Solid Waste Ordinance.

We also recommend that Management review and update every
year the agency’s Solid Waste Management Plan by Municipal Solid
Waste Management Board as mandated under Section 16 of RA 9003.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor:

 require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of all Inventories and Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) accounts of the Municipality so that the corresponding report can be submitted to the Audit Team;  and

 thereafter, instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the resulting balance of the physical count with the accounting records in order to present the correct balances of said accounts in the financial statements.

In the event that deficiencies/discrepancies exist between the Inventory Report and accounting records, the necessary adjustments/corrections should be made. Also, require the Municipal Treasurer to make a report of unserviceable/non-functional PPEs in the IIRUP which could be undertaken simultaneous with the physical inventory for disposal and eventual proper relief of accountabilities.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) for CY 2016 to the Audit Team on June 5, 2017.
observations.reason: Reconciliation made by the Audit Team between the amounts reflected in the trial balance and the RPCPPE revealed an unreonciled difference amounting to P7,800,610.10.  No Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) was submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for all the LGU’s bank accounts to the Audit Team together with the paid checks, debit/credit memos for verification as provided for under Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-011. Also make an extra effort to submit the bank reconciliation statements on time.
observations.managementAction: The Management exerted effort in the timely submission of monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements.
observations.reason: Delay in the submission of monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for CY 2016 was reduced to 126 days.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
 The Municipal Treasurer:

Instruct all collectors to prepare RCDs and remit all collections to the Municipal Treasurer in accordance with Section 29, Vol. 1 of the NGAS Manual;

Be more circumspect in the acknowledgment of remittances in terms of accuracy of the amount;

Refrain from allowing incomplete remittances from the collecting officers.

 The Municipal Accountant:

Ensure the accuracy of the amounts of recorded transactions and adjust the balance of the accounts affected by the miscalculated collections; and 

The practice of charging the unremitted collections to receivable account be refrained as this only encourages delayed remittance and/or incomplete remittance or non-remittance at all the collections receipted by the collecting officers.
observations.managementAction: The Management had already stopped the practice of recording unremitted collections by debiting the account Due from Officers and Employees.
observations.reason: No unremitted collections were observed for CY 2016.  However, delay was still noted in the remittances of collections by the collecting officers for CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 to reflect the correct balance of Trust Liability- DRRM (Account 438) in the Trust Fund book.  Likewise, exert an extra effort to follow the format of disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements as shown in Annex D of same circular. Lastly, instruct the Municipal Accountant to properly classify each transaction so that it will be recorded in their proper accounts.
observations.managementAction: The ICO-Municipal Accountant already made the entries both in the General and Trust Fund to reflect the correct balance of Trust Liability- DRRMF for CY 2015. Also, the disclosure requirements prescribed by COA Circular No. 2012-002 were already complied.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to remit the excess and unutilized PDAF and DAF funds to the Bureau of Treasury and ask Official Receipt for the same pursuant to Supreme Court decisions declaring PDAF and some acts and practices of DAF unconstitutional and in compliance to Section 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.
observations.managementAction: The municipality already remitted the balances of PDAF and DAF to the Bureau of Treasury on December 29, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the contractor to repair the cited deficiencies in the construction or withhold the payment until the project are rectified.
observations.managementAction: The contractors were notified at 2015 once and these deficiencies in the construction were repaired and rectified immediately.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management closely monitor the conditions of its properties and ensure that its use is optimized so as to avoid wastage of government funds.
observations.managementAction:
After being looked at by boat engine mechanics, the pump boat in question has been declared "beyond repair" as assessed and cost of repair has been estimated to cost more that 40% of its value, so the offices concerned (Treasury, GSO, Engineering and MDRRMO) has decided to file a “waste material report”. As to rubber boats, the MDRRM office was able to canvass prices for repair. However, there is only one shop in Cebu that offers “patching only”. Patching involves a cleansing liquid (cost is at least 5,000 per gallon) and patching pads which cost a minimum of 5,000 each according to them. And the unit has to be brought to Cebu. The shop advised that rubber boat repair experts are in Batangas City. Summing up the estimated cost of repair, it would amount to more than 30% of its value so the offices involved also decided to forego the repair and a “waste material report” has been filed also.
Proper monitoring and care of equipments is now being practiced at MDRRM office (as per COA’s recommendation last year)

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) should strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 and see to it that all procurements be made through competitive bidding unless made through conditions for the use of the alternative modes of procurement are so warranted, and to refrain from justifying emergency need or time constraints especially if such services will be utilized on a predetermined date.
observations.managementAction: Upon receipt of the AOM, the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) should strictly adhered to the provisions of RA 9184 and all procurements since then on fiesta related activities were made through a licensed event organizer and for the past 5 years Lemuel Rosos was the sole knowledgeable organizer/ choreographer available in town.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management refund the overpayment of claims on transportation expenses pursuant to EOs 298 and 248 and COA Circular No. 2012-003.
We also recommend that, henceforth, adherence to EOs 298 and 248, COA Circular No. 2012-003 and Section 96(b) of RA 7160 is enjoined to avoid personal liability.
observations.managementAction: Immediately stopped overpayment of claims on transportation expenses.
observations.reason: The Official travelled in Manila, Outside the Cebu Province, without the authority from the Governor contrary to Section 96(b) of RA 7160.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the officials and employees refund the travelling amount spent for taxi fares in excess of the allowable amount pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-005.
We further recommend that henceforth, claims for taxi fare while on official travel shall be supported with official receipt to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: Justified to COA Auditors that the claims on taxi fares being implemented in our agency, we really have not paid for taxi fares even just for the allowable amount of P150.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer submit the duly approved resolution from the Sangguniang Bayan authorizing the opening of the depository to open a bank account other than the ASPAC, a bank authorized other than a GFI so as daily collection can be quickly deposited and comply with the documentary requirements set forth in the Department of Finance Order/Circular 27-05, 001-2015 and 002-2015 otherwise the Municipality must transfer all funds and cash balances to banks that meet the requirements provided in said circular.
observations.managementAction:
Attached is a copy of SB Resolution No. 96-2011 and 115-2011. It is in the interest of the Government Unit of Santa Fe to open a bank account with ASPAC, a bank located in the neighboring town of Bantayan, which is 10 kilometers away from Santa Fe.
ASPAC is just located in the neighboring Department town of Bantayan, which is 10 kilometers away from Santa Fe. The daily collection can be quickly deposited.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management provide guidelines in the grant of financial assistance based on DSWD Omnibus Guidelines on the Management of Operated Crisis Intervention as amended in AO. No.5 series of 2008.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Management has established and implemented the 10 municipal guidelines in the grant of financial assistance to individuals and families in need thru a Municipal Ordinance No. 05-2016 attached herewith.
Basis of extending Financial assistance is DSWD Omnibus Guidelines on the Management of Operated Crisis Intervention as amended in AO. No.5 series of 2008.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the municipal treasurer, being the general service officer of the LGU, to cause the markings on all motor vehicles with "For Official Use Only" and the name of the office using the vehicle in accordance with the provisions of Item V (2) of COA Circular No. 75-6 dated November 7, 1975. Aside from the markings, red plates should be installed thereon.
observations.managementAction: All new purchases of motor vehicles are now marked “For Official Use Only” and red plates are installed.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the officials who authorized the payment of the ESA not in accordance with the Memorandum Circular be held accountable for the irregular payment and that the guidelines in the DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 24, Series of 2014 be strictly followed.
observations.managementAction:
In our capacity as implementing agency of the ESA project, this Management, in the implementation, is abiding, lawfully observing and following the guidelines set by the DSWD in accordance with Memorandum Circular No. 24 series of 2014.
We, the authorized officials, strongly believe that there were no irregularities in the implementation.
observations.reason: We too also received the Emergency Shelter Assistance (ESA) in the total amount of P3,830,000.00 in CY 2015 contrary to the Memorandum Circular No. 24 Series of 2014. The YPDR is not recognized as partner in Local Government Unit in housing project implementation and it didn’t coordinate with the Local Social Welfare Office

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal General Services Officer/Municipal Treasurer conduct a complete physical count of properties, prepare and submit to the Auditor an updated inventory report on the physical count of the properties not later than January 31 of each year pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.
We also recommend that Management assign permanent personnel to give priority in the reconciliation of the records pursuant to Section 114 of NGAS Manual, Volume I.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal General Services and the Municipal Treasury office conducted a complete Physical count of Property, Plant and submit to the Auditor an updated inventory report last January 4-31, 2017.
Reconciliation of records with the Accounting Department is on-going. Last 2015, the LGU was able to submit a list of the Property, Plant and Equipment per Physical Inventory and duly reconciled with the records.
observations.reason: On-going. Last 2015, the LGU was able to submit a list of the Property, Plant and Equipment per Physical Inventory and duly reconciled with the records. PRIO YEAR 2013 Reconciliation of records with the Accounting department is on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Head of Agency submit the Memorandum of Agreement duly executed by the Department of Agriculture and the Municipality of Santa Fe for proper evaluation of the project.
observations.managementAction: Municipal General Services Officer provided supporting documents on the reported damaged and lost items, and the official receipts on return of accountability were already submitted to COA together with reply to AOM No. 16-005 dated 02/01/16 last March 14, 2016.
observations.reason: Relief from accountability of the reported damaged and lost items, and the official receipts on return of accountability were already submitted to COA together with reply to AOM No. 16-005 dated 02/01/16 last March 14, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend Continue the deduction of separated employees from Terminal Leave Pay of said disallowances and that management should limit the granting of additional benefits for personal services within the 55% level for a fourth class municipality and lower as provided for by law and to require all officials and employees to refund the amount of P10,000.00 each.
observations.managementAction:
Continue the deduction of separated employees from Terminal Leave Pay of said disallowances.
Only separated employees have paid the said disallowances.
observations.reason: Only separated employees have paid the said disallowances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management should limit the granting of additional benefit from Terminal Leave Pay within the 55% level for a fifth class municipality and lower as provided for by law and to require all officials and employees to refund the amount of P10,000.00 each.
observations.managementAction: Only separated employees have settled disallowances.
observations.reason: Only separated employees have settled disallowances.

observations.recommendation: The Officer-In-Charge of the Municipal Treasurer’s Office should immediately deposit immediately all collections received by her to prevent losses of government funds.
observations.managementAction: Collections on the last working day of December 2016 is being deposited on the next banking day of January 2017, thus showing a huge cash in vault balance.
observations.reason: Collections on the last working day of December 2016 is being deposited on the next banking day of January 2017, thus showing a huge cash in vault balance.
